title.en: Autobiographical Afterword of the Grand Historian
title.raw: 《太史公自序》 Autobiographical Afterword of the Grand Historian

sentences.zh: 昔在顓頊，命南正重以司天，北正黎以司地。

translations.literal: In ancient times, during the reign of Zhuanxu, he appointed Chong as Director of the South to oversee the heavens, and Li as Director of the North to oversee the earth.
translations.idiomatic: In ancient times, under the reign of Emperor Zhuanxu, Chong was appointed Director of the South to govern the heavens, and Li was appointed Director of the North to govern the earth.

sentences.zh: 唐虞之際，紹重黎之後，使復典之，至于夏商，故重黎氏世序天地。

translations.literal: In the time of Tang and Yu, the descendants of Chong and Li were continued in office, charged once more with overseeing these domains, down through the Xia and Shang; thus the Chongli clan managed heaven and earth through the generations.
translations.idiomatic: During the age of Yao and Shun, the descendants of Chong and Li were continued in office and charged with these same responsibilities down through the Xia and Shang dynasties. Thus the Chongli clan presided over the affairs of heaven and earth through the generations.

sentences.zh: 其在周，程伯休甫其後也。

translations.literal: Under the Zhou, their descendant was Cheng Bo Xiufu.
translations.idiomatic: Under the Zhou dynasty, their line continued through Cheng Bo Xiufu.

sentences.zh: 當周宣王時，失其守而為司馬氏。

translations.literal: In the time of King Xuan of Zhou, they lost their office and became the Sima clan.
translations.idiomatic: During the reign of King Xuan of Zhou, they lost their hereditary office and took the surname Sima.

sentences.zh: 司馬氏世典周史。

translations.literal: The Sima clan served as chroniclers of Zhou through the generations.
translations.idiomatic: The Sima clan served as Zhou's chroniclers through the generations.

sentences.zh: 惠襄之閒，司馬氏去周適晉。

translations.literal: Between the reigns of Kings Hui and Xiang, the Sima clan left Zhou and went to Jin.
translations.idiomatic: Between the reigns of Kings Hui and Xiang, the Sima clan departed Zhou and moved to Jin.

sentences.zh: 晉中軍隨會奔秦，而司馬氏入少梁。

translations.literal: The commander of the Jin central army, Sui Hui, fled to Qin, and the Sima clan entered Shaoliang.
translations.idiomatic: When Sui Hui, commander of Jin's central army, fled to Qin, the Sima clan settled in Shaoliang.

sentences.zh: 自司馬氏去周適晉，分散，或在衛，或在趙，或在秦。

translations.literal: After the Sima clan left Zhou for Jin, they scattered: some went to Wei, some to Zhao, and some to Qin.
translations.idiomatic: After leaving Zhou for Jin, the Sima clan dispersed — some to Wei, some to Zhao, some to Qin.

sentences.zh: 其在衛者，相中山。

translations.literal: Those in Wei served as chancellors of Zhongshan.
translations.idiomatic: Those in Wei served as chancellors of the state of Zhongshan.

sentences.zh: 在趙者，以傳劍論顯，蒯聵其後也。

translations.literal: Those in Zhao became renowned for their discourse on the art of swordsmanship; Kuai Kui was their descendant.
translations.idiomatic: Those in Zhao gained renown through their treatises on swordsmanship. Kuai Kui was their descendant.

sentences.zh: 在秦者名錯，與張儀爭論，於是惠王使錯將伐蜀，遂拔，因而守之。

translations.literal: The one in Qin was named Cuo. He debated with Zhang Yi, and thereupon King Hui sent Cuo to lead an expedition against Shu. He conquered it and was then appointed to defend it.
translations.idiomatic: The one in Qin was named Cuo. He clashed in debate with Zhang Yi, and King Hui sent Cuo to lead an army against Shu. He conquered it and was appointed its governor.

sentences.zh: 錯孫靳，事武安君白起。

translations.literal: Cuo's grandson Jin served under Lord Wu'an, Bai Qi.
translations.idiomatic: Cuo's grandson Jin served under Lord Wu'an — the great general Bai Qi.

sentences.zh: 而少梁更名曰夏陽。

translations.literal: Meanwhile, Shaoliang was renamed Xiayang.
translations.idiomatic: Meanwhile, Shaoliang was renamed Xiayang.

sentences.zh: 靳與武安君阬趙長平軍，還而與之俱賜死杜郵，葬於華池。

translations.literal: Jin joined Lord Wu'an in burying alive the Zhao army at Changping; upon returning, he was ordered to die alongside him at Duyou, and was buried at Huachi.
translations.idiomatic: Jin took part with Lord Wu'an in the mass execution of Zhao's army at Changping. Upon their return, both were ordered to take their own lives at Duyou. Jin was buried at Huachi.

sentences.zh: 靳孫昌，昌為秦主鐵官，當始皇之時。

translations.literal: Jin's grandson Chang served as chief iron official of Qin during the time of the First Emperor.
translations.idiomatic: Jin's grandson Chang served as Qin's chief iron official during the reign of the First Emperor.

sentences.zh: 蒯聵玄孫卬為武信君將而徇朝歌。

translations.literal: Kuai Kui's great-great-grandson Ang served as a general under Lord Wuxin and campaigned through Zhaoge.
translations.idiomatic: Kuai Kui's great-great-grandson Ang served as a general under Lord Wuxin and led a campaign through Zhaoge.

sentences.zh: 諸侯之相王，王卬於殷。

translations.literal: When the feudal lords mutually proclaimed themselves kings, Ang was made King of Yin.
translations.idiomatic: When the feudal lords declared themselves kings, Ang was proclaimed King of Yin.

sentences.zh: 漢之伐楚，卬歸漢，以其地為河內郡。

translations.literal: When the Han attacked Chu, Ang surrendered to Han, and his territory was made into the Commandery of Henei.
translations.idiomatic: When the Han attacked Chu, Ang surrendered to the Han side, and his territory was incorporated as the Commandery of Henei.

sentences.zh: 昌生無澤，無澤為漢市長。

translations.literal: Chang fathered Wuze; Wuze served as a market overseer under the Han.
translations.idiomatic: Chang fathered Wuze, who served as a market overseer under the Han dynasty.

sentences.zh: 無澤生喜，喜為五大夫，卒，皆葬高門。

translations.literal: Wuze fathered Xi; Xi attained the rank of Fifth-Rank Grandee. Upon death, they were all buried at Gaomen.
translations.idiomatic: Wuze fathered Xi, who attained the rank of Fifth-Rank Grandee. Upon their deaths, all were buried at Gaomen.

sentences.zh: 喜生談，談為太史公。

translations.literal: Xi fathered Tan; Tan became the Grand Historian.
translations.idiomatic: Xi fathered Tan, who became the Grand Historian.

sentences.zh: 太史公學天官於唐都，受易於楊何，習道論於黃子。

translations.literal: The Grand Historian studied astronomy under Tang Du, received instruction in the Changes from Yang He, and studied Daoist discourse under Master Huang.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Historian studied astronomy under Tang Du, received instruction in the Book of Changes from Yang He, and studied Daoist philosophy under Master Huang.

sentences.zh: 太史公仕於建元元封之閒，愍學者之不達其意而師悖，乃論六家之要指曰：

translations.literal: The Grand Historian served in office between the Jianyuan and Yuanfeng eras. Distressed that scholars failed to grasp the essential meaning of the schools and that their teachings were muddled, he composed a discussion of the key principles of the Six Schools, saying:
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Historian served in office between the Jianyuan and Yuanfeng eras. Distressed that scholars had failed to grasp the essential meaning of the various schools, and that their teachings had become confused, he set down a discussion of the key principles of the Six Schools:

sentences.zh: 易大傳：「天下一致而百慮，同歸而殊涂。」

translations.literal: The Great Commentary on the Changes says: 'The world arrives at the same destination through a hundred different deliberations, and reaches the same goal by different paths.'
translations.idiomatic: The Great Commentary on the Changes says: 'All under heaven arrives at the same destination through a hundred different deliberations, and reaches the same goal by divergent paths.'

sentences.zh: 夫陰陽、儒、墨、名、法、道德，此務為治者也，直所從言之異路，有省不省耳。

translations.literal: Now the Yin-Yang, Confucian, Mohist, Logician, Legalist, and Daoist schools all strive for good governance; they merely differ in the paths by which they speak, some being more and others less perceptive.
translations.idiomatic: The Yin-Yang, Confucian, Mohist, Logician, Legalist, and Daoist schools all strive for good governance. They merely differ in the paths by which they approach the subject — some more perceptive, others less so.

sentences.zh: 嘗竊觀陰陽之術，大祥而眾忌諱，使人拘而多所畏；

translations.literal: I have ventured to observe the arts of the Yin-Yang school: they are full of auspicious signs yet burdened with countless taboos, making people constrained and fearful of many things;
translations.idiomatic: I have ventured to observe the methods of the Yin-Yang school. They are laden with auspicious signs yet encumbered by countless taboos, leaving people hemmed in and fearful;

sentences.zh: 然其序四時之大順，不可失也。

translations.literal: yet their ordering of the great cycle of the four seasons must not be neglected.
translations.idiomatic: yet their ordering of the great cycle of the four seasons is something that must not be lost.

sentences.zh: 儒者博而寡要，勞而少功，是以其事難盡從；

translations.literal: The Confucians are broad but lack focus, laborious yet of little practical effect; therefore it is difficult to follow their teachings in full;
translations.idiomatic: The Confucians are broad in scope but lack concision, laborious yet yielding little practical result. For this reason, it is difficult to follow their teachings in full;

sentences.zh: 然其序君臣父子之禮，列夫婦長幼之別，不可易也。

translations.literal: yet their ordering of the rites between ruler and minister, father and son, and their ranking of the distinctions between husband and wife, elder and younger, cannot be changed.
translations.idiomatic: yet their ordering of the rites between ruler and minister, father and son, and their distinctions between husband and wife, elder and younger, cannot be altered.

sentences.zh: 墨者儉而難遵，是以其事不可遍循；

translations.literal: The Mohists are frugal but hard to follow; therefore their teachings cannot be adopted in their entirety;
translations.idiomatic: The Mohists are frugal, but their standards are difficult to maintain. For this reason, their teachings cannot be adopted in full;

sentences.zh: 然其彊本節用，不可廢也。

translations.literal: yet their emphasis on strengthening the root and economizing on expenditure must not be discarded.
translations.idiomatic: yet their emphasis on strengthening fundamentals and economizing expenditure must not be discarded.

sentences.zh: 法家嚴而少恩；

translations.literal: The Legalists are strict but show little kindness;
translations.idiomatic: The Legalists are strict but lack compassion;

sentences.zh: 然其正君臣上下之分，不可改矣。

translations.literal: yet their rectification of the distinctions between ruler and minister, superior and inferior, cannot be changed.
translations.idiomatic: yet their rectification of the distinctions between ruler and minister, superior and inferior, cannot be altered.

sentences.zh: 名家使人儉而善失真；

translations.literal: The Logicians make people exacting but prone to losing sight of the truth;
translations.idiomatic: The Logicians make people meticulous but prone to losing sight of substance;

sentences.zh: 然其正名實，不可不察也。

translations.literal: yet their rectification of names and actualities must not go unexamined.
translations.idiomatic: yet their rectification of names and realities must not be overlooked.

sentences.zh: 道家使人精神專一，動合無形，贍足萬物。

translations.literal: The Daoists enable one's spirit to become unified and concentrated, one's actions to harmonize with the formless, and one's resources to sustain all things.
translations.idiomatic: The Daoists enable the spirit to become unified and concentrated, action to accord with the formless, and resources to sustain all creation.

sentences.zh: 其為術也，因陰陽之大順，采儒墨之善，撮名法之要，與時遷移，應物變化，立俗施事，無所不宜，指約而易操，事少而功多。

translations.literal: As a method, it follows the great cycle of yin and yang, draws on the best of the Confucians and Mohists, extracts the essentials of the Logicians and Legalists, shifts and adapts with the times, responds to the transformation of things, establishes customs and carries out affairs with nothing that is not fitting. Its principles are concise and easy to practice; the effort required is small but the results are great.
translations.idiomatic: As a method, Daoism follows the great cycle of yin and yang, draws on the best of Confucianism and Mohism, extracts the essentials of the Logicians and Legalists, shifts with the times, responds to the transformation of things, and manages customs and affairs with nothing left unfitting. Its principles are concise and easy to practice, requiring little effort yet yielding great results.

sentences.zh: 儒者則不然。

translations.literal: The Confucians, however, are not like this.
translations.idiomatic: The Confucians, on the other hand, are quite different.

sentences.zh: 以為人主天下之儀表也，主倡而臣和，主先而臣隨。

translations.literal: They consider the ruler to be the model for all under heaven: the ruler initiates and the ministers harmonize; the ruler leads and the ministers follow.
translations.idiomatic: They regard the ruler as the exemplar for all under heaven: the ruler initiates and the ministers harmonize; the ruler leads and the ministers follow.

sentences.zh: 如此則主勞而臣逸。

translations.literal: Under such a system, the ruler is exhausted while the ministers are at ease.
translations.idiomatic: Under such a system, the ruler is exhausted while the ministers are at leisure.

sentences.zh: 至於大道之要，去健羨，絀聰明，釋此而任術。

translations.literal: As for the essentials of the Great Way: abandon forceful striving and envy, set aside cleverness and intelligence, let go of these things and rely instead on method.
translations.idiomatic: The essentials of the Great Way are these: abandon forceful striving and envy, set aside cleverness and keen intelligence, let go of these and rely on method.

sentences.zh: 夫神大用則竭，形大勞則敝。

translations.literal: When the spirit is greatly exerted, it becomes exhausted; when the body is greatly labored, it breaks down.
translations.idiomatic: When the spirit is overexerted, it becomes exhausted; when the body is overworked, it breaks down.

sentences.zh: 形神騷動，欲與天地長久，非所聞也。

translations.literal: If both body and spirit are in turmoil, to wish to endure as long as heaven and earth — this is something unheard of.
translations.idiomatic: When body and spirit are both in turmoil, to hope to endure as long as heaven and earth is unheard of.

sentences.zh: 夫陰陽四時、八位、十二度、二十四節各有教令，順之者昌，逆之者不死則亡，未必然也，故曰「使人拘而多畏」。

translations.literal: Now the Yin-Yang school teaches that the four seasons, eight positions, twelve degrees, and twenty-four solar terms each have their regulations: those who obey them will prosper, and those who defy them will if not die then perish — but this is not necessarily so. Therefore it is said that they 'make people constrained and fearful.'
translations.idiomatic: The Yin-Yang school teaches that the four seasons, eight positions, twelve degrees, and twenty-four solar terms each carry their own regulations: obey them and you prosper; defy them and you perish. But this is not necessarily so. Hence it is said they 'make people constrained and fearful.'

sentences.zh: 夫春生夏長，秋收冬藏，此天道之大經也，弗順則無以為天下綱紀，故曰「四時之大順，不可失也」。

translations.literal: That spring gives birth, summer nurtures growth, autumn gathers the harvest, and winter stores it away — this is the great constant of the Way of Heaven. If one does not follow it, there is no way to establish the guiding principles for all under heaven. Therefore it is said that 'the great cycle of the four seasons must not be lost.'
translations.idiomatic: That spring gives birth, summer nurtures growth, autumn gathers the harvest, and winter stores it away — this is the great constant of Heaven's Way. Without it, there is no foundation for governing the realm. Hence it is said: 'the great cycle of the four seasons must not be lost.'

sentences.zh: 夫儒者以六藝為法。

translations.literal: The Confucians take the Six Arts as their standard.
translations.idiomatic: The Confucians take the Six Arts as their guiding standard.

sentences.zh: 六藝經傳以千萬數，累世不能通其學，當年不能究其禮，故曰「博而寡要，勞而少功」。

translations.literal: The classics and commentaries of the Six Arts number in the tens of thousands; successive generations cannot master their learning, and a single lifetime cannot exhaust their rites. Therefore it is said they are 'broad but lack focus, laborious yet of little practical effect.'
translations.idiomatic: The classics and commentaries of the Six Arts number in the tens of thousands. Successive generations cannot master their learning, and a single lifetime cannot fathom all their rites. Hence it is said they are 'broad but lack concision, laborious yet yielding little result.'

sentences.zh: 若夫列君臣父子之禮，序夫婦長幼之別，雖百家弗能易也。

translations.literal: But as for their ordering of the rites between ruler and minister, father and son, and their arrangement of the distinctions between husband and wife, elder and younger — not even a hundred schools could change these.
translations.idiomatic: Yet when it comes to their ordering of the rites between ruler and minister, father and son, and the distinctions between husband and wife, elder and younger — not even a hundred rival schools could alter these.

sentences.zh: 墨者亦尚堯舜道，言其德行曰：「堂高三尺，土階三等，茅茨不翦，采椽不刮。

translations.literal: The Mohists also revere the Way of Yao and Shun, describing their virtuous conduct thus: 'The hall was three chi high, the earthen steps had three levels, the thatched roof was left untrimmed, and the rafters of wild oak were left unplaned.
translations.idiomatic: The Mohists also revere the Way of Yao and Shun, describing their virtuous conduct as follows: 'The hall stood only three chi high, the earthen steps had but three levels, the thatched roof was left untrimmed, and the oak rafters were left unplaned.

sentences.zh: 食土簋，啜土刑，糲粱之食，藜霍之羹。

translations.literal: They ate from earthenware bowls, sipped from clay cups, ate coarse millet, and supped on pigweed and bean-leaf soup.
translations.idiomatic: They ate from earthenware bowls, drank from clay cups, subsisted on coarse millet, and supped on pigweed and bean-leaf broth.

sentences.zh: 夏日葛衣，冬日鹿裘。」

translations.literal: In summer they wore hemp cloth, in winter deerskin.'
translations.idiomatic: In summer they wore hemp cloth; in winter, deerskin.'

sentences.zh: 其送死，桐棺三寸，舉音不盡其哀。

translations.literal: For burying the dead: a paulownia coffin three cun thick, and the mourning cries did not fully express their grief.
translations.idiomatic: For burial: a paulownia coffin three cun thick, and mourning cries that did not fully express their grief.

sentences.zh: 教喪禮，必以此為萬民之率。

translations.literal: They taught funeral rites and insisted on making these the standard for all the people.
translations.idiomatic: They taught funeral rites, insisting that these be the standard for all the people.

sentences.zh: 使天下法若此，則尊卑無別也。

translations.literal: If the entire realm were made to follow such standards, there would be no distinction between the noble and the lowly.
translations.idiomatic: Were the entire realm made to follow such standards, all distinctions between noble and humble would be erased.

sentences.zh: 夫世異時移，事業不必同，故曰「儉而難遵」。

translations.literal: Now the world changes and times shift; enterprises need not remain the same. Therefore it is said they are 'frugal but difficult to follow.'
translations.idiomatic: The world changes and times shift; one's way of life need not remain the same. Hence it is said they are 'frugal but difficult to maintain.'

sentences.zh: 要曰彊本節用，則人給家足之道也。

translations.literal: In essence, strengthening the root and economizing expenditure — this is the way to ensure that individuals are provided for and families are self-sufficient.
translations.idiomatic: In essence, strengthening fundamentals and economizing expenditure is the path to ensuring that every individual is provided for and every household self-sufficient.

sentences.zh: 此墨子之所長，雖百長弗能廢也。

translations.literal: This is the strength of Mozi, and even a hundred schools' strengths could not abolish it.
translations.idiomatic: This is the enduring strength of Mozi — one that not even the combined strengths of a hundred schools could abolish.

sentences.zh: 法家不別親疏，不殊貴賤，一斷於法，則親親尊尊之恩絕矣。

translations.literal: The Legalists make no distinction between the close and the distant, no differentiation between the noble and the base; they decide everything by law alone, and so the bonds of affection between kin and the respect for the honored are severed.
translations.idiomatic: The Legalists draw no distinction between close and distant, noble and base. They judge everything by law alone, severing the bonds of affection between kin and the respect due to the honored.

sentences.zh: 可以行一時之計，而不可長用也，故曰「嚴而少恩」。

translations.literal: This may serve as a temporary expedient but cannot be used permanently. Therefore it is said they are 'strict but show little kindness.'
translations.idiomatic: Such methods may serve as a temporary expedient, but they cannot be sustained. Hence it is said they are 'strict but lack compassion.'

sentences.zh: 若尊主卑臣，明分職不得相踰越，雖百家弗能改也。

translations.literal: But as for honoring the ruler and humbling the minister, clarifying the division of duties so that no one may overstep — not even a hundred schools could change this.
translations.idiomatic: Yet when it comes to honoring the ruler and humbling the minister, and clearly demarcating duties so that none may overstep — not even a hundred rival schools could alter this.

sentences.zh: 名家苛察繳繞，使人不得反其意，專決於名而失人情，故曰「使人儉而善失真」。

translations.literal: The Logicians are harshly scrutinizing and entangling, preventing people from returning to their original intent; they fixate on names and lose touch with human feeling. Therefore it is said they 'make people meticulous but prone to losing sight of the truth.'
translations.idiomatic: The Logicians are harshly pedantic and circuitous, preventing people from grasping the original intent. They fixate on names and lose touch with human feeling. Hence it is said they 'make people meticulous but prone to losing substance.'

sentences.zh: 若夫控名責實，參伍不失，此不可不察也。

translations.literal: But as for controlling names and demanding corresponding actualities, cross-checking without error — this must not go unexamined.
translations.idiomatic: Yet when it comes to aligning names with realities, cross-checking without error — this must not be neglected.

sentences.zh: 道家無為，又曰無不為，其實易行，其辭難知。

translations.literal: The Daoists advocate non-action, yet also say there is nothing they do not accomplish. In practice it is easy to follow, but in words it is hard to understand.
translations.idiomatic: The Daoists advocate non-action, yet claim there is nothing they cannot accomplish. In practice their way is easy to follow, but in words it is difficult to grasp.

sentences.zh: 其術以虛無為本，以因循為用。

translations.literal: Their method takes emptiness and nothingness as its foundation, and following the natural course as its application.
translations.idiomatic: Their method takes emptiness and nothingness as its foundation, and working with the natural course of things as its application.

sentences.zh: 無成埶，無常形，故能究萬物之情。

translations.literal: It has no fixed configuration and no constant form; therefore it can fathom the nature of all ten thousand things.
translations.idiomatic: Having no fixed configuration and no constant form, it is able to fathom the nature of all things.

sentences.zh: 不為物先，不為物後，故能為萬物主。

translations.literal: It does not go before things, nor does it lag behind them; therefore it can be master of all things.
translations.idiomatic: It neither precedes things nor follows after them, and so it can be master of all things.

sentences.zh: 有法無法，因時為業；

translations.literal: Whether there is a fixed method or no method, it acts according to the demands of the time;
translations.idiomatic: Whether employing method or abandoning it, it acts in accordance with the demands of the moment;

sentences.zh: 有度無度，因物與合。

translations.literal: whether there is a fixed measure or no measure, it adapts and merges with things.
translations.idiomatic: whether applying measure or discarding it, it adapts and merges with things as they arise.

sentences.zh: 故曰「聖人不朽，時變是守。

translations.literal: Therefore it is said: 'The sage does not remain fixed; he holds to the changes of the times.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore it is said: 'The sage does not cling to permanence; he holds fast to the changes of the times.

sentences.zh: 虛者道之常也，因者君之綱」也。

translations.literal: Emptiness is the constant of the Way, and following the natural course is the guiding principle of the ruler.'
translations.idiomatic: Emptiness is the constant of the Way, and following the natural course is the ruler's guiding principle.'

sentences.zh: 群臣并至，使各自明也。

translations.literal: When all the ministers come together, each is made to reveal himself clearly.
translations.idiomatic: When all the ministers present themselves, each is made to reveal his true nature.

sentences.zh: 其實中其聲者謂之端，實不中其聲者謂之窾。

translations.literal: When reality matches the claim, it is called 'upright'; when reality does not match the claim, it is called 'hollow.'
translations.idiomatic: When substance matches the claim, it is called 'upright.' When substance does not match the claim, it is called 'hollow.'

sentences.zh: 窾言不聽，姦乃不生，賢不肖自分，白黑乃形。

translations.literal: When hollow words are not heeded, wickedness does not arise; the worthy and the unworthy distinguish themselves, and right and wrong become manifest.
translations.idiomatic: When hollow words go unheeded, wickedness does not arise. The worthy and the unworthy reveal themselves, and right and wrong stand clearly defined.

sentences.zh: 在所欲用耳，何事不成。

translations.literal: It depends only on what one wishes to employ — what affair cannot be accomplished?
translations.idiomatic: It depends only on what one chooses to employ. What affair could fail to be accomplished?

sentences.zh: 乃合大道，混混冥冥。

translations.literal: This accords with the Great Way — vast, dim, and obscure.
translations.idiomatic: This accords with the Great Way — vast, deep, and unfathomable.

sentences.zh: 光燿天下，復反無名。

translations.literal: It illuminates all under heaven, then returns to namelessness.
translations.idiomatic: It illuminates all under heaven, then returns to the nameless.

sentences.zh: 凡人所生者神也，所託者形也。

translations.literal: What gives life to a person is the spirit; what the spirit entrusts itself to is the body.
translations.idiomatic: What gives life to a person is the spirit; what the spirit lodges in is the body.

sentences.zh: 神大用則竭，形大勞則敝，形神離則死。

translations.literal: When the spirit is greatly exerted it becomes exhausted; when the body is greatly labored it breaks down; when body and spirit separate, death follows.
translations.idiomatic: When the spirit is overexerted, it becomes exhausted. When the body is overworked, it breaks down. When body and spirit part ways, death follows.

sentences.zh: 死者不可復生，離者不可復反，故聖人重之。

translations.literal: The dead cannot be brought back to life, and the separated cannot be reunited; therefore the sage treats this with the utmost gravity.
translations.idiomatic: The dead cannot be brought back to life, nor can what has separated be reunited. The sage therefore treats this matter with the utmost gravity.

sentences.zh: 由是觀之，神者生之本也，形者生之具也。

translations.literal: Viewed from this perspective, the spirit is the root of life, and the body is life's vessel.
translations.idiomatic: Seen in this light, the spirit is the root of life, and the body is its vessel.

sentences.zh: 不先定其神［形］，而曰「我有以治天下」，何由哉？

translations.literal: If one does not first settle one's spirit and body, and yet says 'I have the means to govern the realm' — on what basis could this be?
translations.idiomatic: If one does not first settle one's own spirit and body, yet claims to have the means to govern the realm — on what basis?

sentences.zh: 太史公既掌天官，不治民。

translations.literal: The Grand Historian had charge of celestial affairs and did not govern the people.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Historian was in charge of celestial affairs and did not administer the people.

sentences.zh: 有子曰遷。

translations.literal: He had a son named Qian.
translations.idiomatic: He had a son named Qian.

sentences.zh: 遷生龍門，耕牧河山之陽。

translations.literal: Qian was born at Longmen and grew up farming and herding on the southern slopes of the river and mountains.
translations.idiomatic: Qian was born at Longmen. He grew up farming and herding on the sunny southern slopes of the river and the mountains.

sentences.zh: 年十歲則誦古文。

translations.literal: By the age of ten he was reciting the ancient texts.
translations.idiomatic: By the age of ten, he could recite the ancient texts.

sentences.zh: 二十而南游江、淮，上會稽，探禹穴，闚九疑，浮於沅、湘；

translations.literal: At twenty he traveled south to the Yangtze and Huai rivers, ascended Mount Kuaiji, explored Yu's Cave, gazed upon the Nine Yi peaks, and floated down the Yuan and Xiang rivers;
translations.idiomatic: At twenty, he journeyed south to the Yangtze and the Huai, climbed Mount Kuaiji, explored the Cave of Yu, gazed upon the Nine Yi peaks, and drifted down the Yuan and Xiang rivers;

sentences.zh: 北涉汶、泗，講業齊、魯之都，觀孔子之遺風，鄉射鄒、嶧；

translations.literal: to the north he crossed the Wen and Si rivers, studied at the capitals of Qi and Lu, observed the legacy of Confucius, and practiced the archery ceremonies at Zou and Yi;
translations.idiomatic: to the north, he crossed the Wen and Si rivers, pursued his studies at the capitals of Qi and Lu, observed the lingering influence of Confucius, and took part in the archery ceremonies at Zou and Yi;

sentences.zh: 戹困鄱、薛、彭城，過梁、楚以歸。

translations.literal: he suffered hardship at Po, Xue, and Pengcheng, then passed through Liang and Chu before returning home.
translations.idiomatic: he endured hardship at Po, Xue, and Pengcheng, then passed through Liang and Chu on his way home.

sentences.zh: 於是遷仕為郎中，奉使西征巴、蜀以南，南略邛、笮、昆明，還報命。

translations.literal: Thereupon Qian entered government service as a Gentleman of the Palace, and was sent on a mission westward to Ba, Shu, and the lands to the south, traveling through Qiong, Zuo, and Kunming, before returning to report.
translations.idiomatic: Qian then entered government service as a Gentleman of the Palace. He was dispatched on a mission westward through Ba and Shu and the lands to their south, traversing Qiong, Zuo, and Kunming, before returning to report.

sentences.zh: 是歲天子始建漢家之封，而太史公留滯周南，不得與從事，故發憤且卒。

translations.literal: That year the Son of Heaven first performed the feng and shan sacrifices for the Han house, but the Grand Historian was stranded south of Zhou and could not take part. Overcome with frustration, he fell ill and died.
translations.idiomatic: That year the Son of Heaven performed the feng and shan sacrifices for the first time in the Han dynasty, but the Grand Historian was stranded south of Zhou and could not participate. Consumed by frustration and resentment, he fell ill and died.

sentences.zh: 而子遷適使反，見父於河洛之閒。

translations.literal: His son Qian happened to return from a mission and saw his father in the region between the Yellow River and the Luo.
translations.idiomatic: His son Qian happened to return from a mission just then, and found his father in the region between the Yellow River and the Luo.

sentences.zh: 太史公執遷手而泣曰：「余先周室之太史也。

translations.literal: The Grand Historian grasped Qian's hand and wept, saying: 'Our ancestors were Grand Historians of the Zhou court.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Historian grasped Qian's hand and wept. 'Our ancestors were Grand Historians of the Zhou court,' he said.

sentences.zh: 自上世嘗顯功名於虞夏，典天官事。

translations.literal: From the earliest ages they distinguished themselves in service under Yu and Xia, overseeing the affairs of the celestial office.
translations.idiomatic: From the earliest ages, they won renown in the service of Yu and Xia, presiding over the celestial office.

sentences.zh: 後世中衰，絕於予乎？

translations.literal: In later generations our line declined. Will it end with me?
translations.idiomatic: In later generations our line has declined. Is it to end with me?

sentences.zh: 汝復為太史，則續吾祖矣。

translations.literal: If you in turn become Grand Historian, you will continue the work of our ancestors.
translations.idiomatic: If you become Grand Historian in your turn, you will carry forward the work of our forebears.

sentences.zh: 今天子接千歲之統，封泰山，而余不得從行，是命也夫，命也夫！

translations.literal: Now the Son of Heaven has received the succession of a thousand years and performed the feng sacrifice at Mount Tai, yet I was not able to attend. This is fate — fate!
translations.idiomatic: Now the Son of Heaven has received the succession of a thousand years and performed the feng sacrifice at Mount Tai, yet I could not be there. This is fate! Fate!

sentences.zh: 余死，汝必為太史；

translations.literal: When I die, you must become the Grand Historian;
translations.idiomatic: After I die, you must become the Grand Historian.

sentences.zh: 為太史，無忘吾所欲論著矣。

translations.literal: as Grand Historian, do not forget what I wished to discuss and compose.
translations.idiomatic: As Grand Historian, never forget what I wished to discuss and compose.

sentences.zh: 且夫孝始於事親，中於事君，終於立身。

translations.literal: Moreover, filial piety begins with serving one's parents, continues with serving one's sovereign, and culminates in establishing oneself.
translations.idiomatic: Filial piety begins with serving one's parents, continues in serving one's sovereign, and finds its fulfillment in establishing oneself.

sentences.zh: 揚名於後世，以顯父母，此孝之大者。

translations.literal: To make one's name known to posterity and thereby bring glory to one's parents — this is the greatest expression of filial piety.
translations.idiomatic: To make one's name known to future generations and thereby bring glory to one's parents — this is the highest expression of filial piety.

sentences.zh: 夫天下稱誦周公，言其能論歌文武之德，宣周邵之風，達太王王季之思慮，爰及公劉，以尊后稷也。

translations.literal: The whole realm praises and recites the Duke of Zhou, saying that he was able to compose songs extolling the virtue of Kings Wen and Wu, to proclaim the influence of the Dukes of Zhou and Shao, to convey the thoughts and concerns of the Great King and King Ji, extending back to Gongliu, in order to honor Hou Ji.
translations.idiomatic: All the realm praises and recites the Duke of Zhou, for he composed songs extolling the virtue of Kings Wen and Wu, proclaimed the legacy of the Dukes of Zhou and Shao, conveyed the aspirations of the Great King and King Ji, and traced the line back through Gongliu to honor Hou Ji.

sentences.zh: 幽厲之後，王道缺，禮樂衰，孔子修舊起廢，論詩書，作春秋，則學者至今則之。

translations.literal: After Kings You and Li, the kingly Way fell into disrepair and rites and music declined. Confucius restored what was old and revived what had been abandoned, discoursed on the Odes and Documents, and composed the Spring and Autumn Annals — and scholars to this day take him as their model.
translations.idiomatic: After Kings You and Li, the kingly Way fell into disrepair, and rites and music declined. Confucius restored what was old, revived what had been abandoned, expounded the Odes and the Documents, and composed the Spring and Autumn Annals. Scholars take him as their standard to this very day.

sentences.zh: 自獲麟以來四百有餘歲，而諸侯相兼，史記放絕。

translations.literal: It has been more than four hundred years since the capture of the unicorn, and during this time the feudal lords have swallowed one another up, and the historical records have been scattered and lost.
translations.idiomatic: More than four hundred years have passed since the capture of the unicorn. In that time the feudal lords have devoured one another, and the historical records have been scattered and lost.

sentences.zh: 今漢興，海內一統，明主賢君忠臣死義之士，余為太史而弗論載，廢天下之史文，余甚懼焉，汝其念哉！」

translations.literal: Now that the Han has risen and the realm is unified — the enlightened sovereigns, worthy rulers, loyal ministers, and gentlemen who died for righteousness — I as Grand Historian have failed to discuss and record them, and the historical writings of the realm have been allowed to lapse. I am deeply fearful of this. You must keep this in mind!'
translations.idiomatic: Now the Han has risen and the realm is unified. There have been enlightened sovereigns, worthy rulers, loyal ministers, and men who died for righteousness — yet I, as Grand Historian, have failed to record and discuss them. The historical writings of the realm have been allowed to perish. I am deeply afraid. You must take this to heart!'

sentences.zh: 遷俯首流涕曰：「小子不敏，請悉論先人所次舊聞，弗敢闕。」

translations.literal: Qian bowed his head, tears streaming down his face, and said: 'Your unworthy son, though lacking in ability, begs to set forth in full the old traditions that our ancestors arranged. I shall not dare to leave any gap.'
translations.idiomatic: Qian bowed his head, tears streaming down his face, and replied: 'Though your unworthy son lacks ability, I beg leave to set forth in full the old traditions our forebears compiled. I shall not dare leave a single gap.'

sentences.zh: 卒三歲而遷為太史令，紬史記石室金匱之書。

translations.literal: Three years after his father's death, Qian was appointed Grand Historian and began to collect and edit the historical documents from the stone chambers and bronze coffers.
translations.idiomatic: Three years after his father's death, Qian was appointed Grand Historian. He set about collecting and editing the historical documents preserved in the stone chambers and bronze coffers.

sentences.zh: 五年而當太初元年，十一月甲子朔旦冬至，天歷始改，建於明堂，諸神受紀。

translations.literal: Five years later came the first year of the Taichu era: the winter solstice fell on the first day of the eleventh month, a jiazi day. The celestial calendar was reformed for the first time, and it was established in the Bright Hall, with all the spirits receiving the new reckoning.
translations.idiomatic: Five years later came the first year of the Taichu era. The winter solstice fell on the first day of the eleventh month, a jiazi day. The celestial calendar was reformed, promulgated in the Bright Hall, and all the spirits received the new reckoning.

sentences.zh: 太史公曰：「先人有言：『自周公卒五百歲而有孔子。

translations.literal: The Grand Historian said: 'My late father used to say: "Five hundred years after the death of the Duke of Zhou, there appeared Confucius.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Historian said: 'My late father used to say: "Five hundred years after the death of the Duke of Zhou, Confucius appeared.

sentences.zh: 孔子卒後至於今五百歲，有能紹明世，正易傳，繼春秋，本詩書禮樂之際？』

translations.literal: Five hundred years have now passed since the death of Confucius. Will there be one who can carry forward this illustrious age, correct the traditions of the Changes, continue the Spring and Autumn Annals, and ground himself in the juncture between the Odes, Documents, Rites, and Music?"
translations.idiomatic: Five hundred years have now passed since Confucius died. Will there be one who can carry forward this illustrious age, correct the traditions of the Changes, continue the Spring and Autumn Annals, and ground his work at the intersection of the Odes, the Documents, the Rites, and Music?"

sentences.zh: 意在斯乎！

translations.literal: His intention lay in this!
translations.idiomatic: His meaning was clear!

sentences.zh: 意在斯乎！

translations.literal: His intention lay in this!
translations.idiomatic: His meaning was unmistakable!

sentences.zh: 小子何敢讓焉。」

translations.literal: How dare I, his unworthy son, decline this charge.'
translations.idiomatic: How could I, his unworthy son, dare to decline this charge?'

sentences.zh: 上大夫壺遂曰：「昔孔子何為而作春秋哉？」

translations.literal: The Senior Grandee Hu Sui asked: 'In ancient times, what prompted Confucius to compose the Spring and Autumn Annals?'
translations.idiomatic: The Senior Grandee Hu Sui asked: 'What was it that moved Confucius to compose the Spring and Autumn Annals?'

sentences.zh: 太史公曰：「余聞董生曰：『周道衰廢，孔子為魯司寇，諸侯害之，大夫壅之。

translations.literal: The Grand Historian replied: 'I have heard Master Dong say: "When the Zhou Way declined and fell into ruin, Confucius served as Minister of Justice of Lu. The feudal lords resented him, and the grandees obstructed him.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Historian replied: 'I heard Master Dong say: "When the Way of Zhou declined and fell into ruin, Confucius was serving as Minister of Justice of Lu. The feudal lords resented him, and the grandees blocked him.

sentences.zh: 孔子知言之不用，道之不行也，是非二百四十二年之中，以為天下儀表，貶天子，退諸侯，討大夫，以達王事而已矣。』

translations.literal: Confucius knew that his words would not be heeded and his Way would not prevail. So he pronounced judgment on right and wrong across two hundred and forty-two years, making this a standard for all under heaven: censuring the Son of Heaven, demoting feudal lords, calling grandees to account — all to convey the affairs of the true king."
translations.idiomatic: Knowing his counsel would not be heeded and his Way would never prevail, Confucius rendered judgment on right and wrong across two hundred and forty-two years, establishing a standard for all under heaven — censuring the Son of Heaven, demoting feudal lords, calling grandees to account — all to fulfill the kingly enterprise."

sentences.zh: 子曰：『我欲載之空言，不如見之於行事之深切著明也。』

translations.literal: Confucius said: "Rather than setting down empty words, it is better to make things manifest through the events of history, where they are more deeply incisive and clearly displayed."
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said: "Rather than setting down empty words, it is better to reveal the truth through actual events, where the lesson is more deeply felt and clearly illuminated."

sentences.zh: 夫春秋，上明三王之道，下辨人事之紀，別嫌疑，明是非，定猶豫，善善惡惡，賢賢賤不肖，存亡國，繼絕世，補敝起廢，王道之大者也。

translations.literal: The Spring and Autumn Annals, above, illuminate the Way of the Three Kings; below, they distinguish the principles of human affairs. They discriminate what is doubtful, clarify right and wrong, settle what is uncertain, approve the good and condemn the wicked, honor the worthy and despise the unworthy, preserve states that have perished, continue lines that have been cut off, repair what is worn and revive what has been abandoned — this is the grandest expression of the kingly Way.
translations.idiomatic: The Spring and Autumn Annals, from above, illuminate the Way of the Three Kings; from below, they discern the principles governing human affairs. They distinguish the doubtful, clarify right from wrong, settle the uncertain, commend the good and condemn the wicked, honor the worthy and despise the unworthy, preserve fallen states, continue severed bloodlines, and repair what is worn and revive what has been abandoned. This is the grandest expression of the kingly Way.

sentences.zh: 易著天地陰陽四時五行，故長於變；

translations.literal: The Changes sets forth heaven and earth, yin and yang, the four seasons and the five phases; therefore it excels in understanding transformation;
translations.idiomatic: The Changes sets forth heaven and earth, yin and yang, the four seasons and five phases. It therefore excels in illuminating transformation;

sentences.zh: 禮經紀人倫，故長於行；

translations.literal: the Rites orders human relationships; therefore it excels in guiding conduct;
translations.idiomatic: the Rites orders the bonds of human relationships, and therefore excels in guiding conduct;

sentences.zh: 書記先王之事，故長於政；

translations.literal: the Documents records the affairs of the former kings; therefore it excels in governance;
translations.idiomatic: the Documents records the affairs of the former kings, and therefore excels in governance;

sentences.zh: 詩記山川谿谷禽獸草木牝牡雌雄，故長於風；

translations.literal: the Odes records mountains, rivers, streams, valleys, birds, beasts, plants, and trees, male and female; therefore it excels in conveying customs and sentiments;
translations.idiomatic: the Odes records mountains and rivers, valleys and streams, birds and beasts, plants and grasses, male and female. It therefore excels in conveying customs and sentiments;

sentences.zh: 樂樂所以立，故長於和；

translations.literal: Music establishes the basis of joy; therefore it excels in producing harmony;
translations.idiomatic: Music establishes the foundation of joy, and therefore excels in producing harmony;

sentences.zh: 春秋辯是非，故長於治人。

translations.literal: the Spring and Autumn Annals distinguishes right from wrong; therefore it excels in governing the people.
translations.idiomatic: the Spring and Autumn Annals distinguishes right from wrong, and therefore excels in governing the people.

sentences.zh: 是故禮以節人，樂以發和，書以道事，詩以達意，易以道化，春秋以道義。

translations.literal: Therefore the Rites regulates people, Music brings forth harmony, the Documents conveys affairs, the Odes communicates intentions, the Changes conveys transformation, and the Spring and Autumn Annals conveys righteousness.
translations.idiomatic: And so the Rites regulates human conduct, Music gives expression to harmony, the Documents conveys affairs of state, the Odes communicates the people's sentiments, the Changes illuminates transformation, and the Spring and Autumn Annals sets forth righteousness.

sentences.zh: 撥亂世反之正，莫近於春秋。

translations.literal: For setting a disordered age aright and returning it to correctness, nothing comes closer than the Spring and Autumn Annals.
translations.idiomatic: For setting a disordered age aright and restoring it to the correct path, nothing comes closer than the Spring and Autumn Annals.

sentences.zh: 春秋文成數萬，其指數千。

translations.literal: The text of the Spring and Autumn Annals runs to tens of thousands of characters, and its essential principles number in the thousands.
translations.idiomatic: The text of the Spring and Autumn Annals runs to tens of thousands of characters, its guiding principles numbering in the thousands.

sentences.zh: 萬物之散聚皆在春秋。

translations.literal: The dispersal and gathering of all things are all found in the Spring and Autumn Annals.
translations.idiomatic: The rise and fall of all things under heaven are contained within the Spring and Autumn Annals.

sentences.zh: 春秋之中，弒君三十六，亡國五十二，諸侯奔走不得保其社稷者不可勝數。

translations.literal: Within the Spring and Autumn period, thirty-six rulers were assassinated, fifty-two states perished, and the number of feudal lords who fled and could not preserve their altars of state is beyond counting.
translations.idiomatic: Within the period covered by the Spring and Autumn Annals, thirty-six rulers were assassinated, fifty-two states perished, and the feudal lords who fled and failed to preserve their altars of state are beyond counting.

sentences.zh: 察其所以，皆失其本已。

translations.literal: If one examines the causes, they all lost sight of their fundamental principles.
translations.idiomatic: Examine the causes, and in every case they had lost sight of the fundamentals.

sentences.zh: 故易曰『失之豪釐，差以千里』。

translations.literal: Therefore the Changes says: 'An error of a hair's breadth leads to a divergence of a thousand li.'
translations.idiomatic: Hence the Changes says: 'An error of a hair's breadth leads astray by a thousand li.'

sentences.zh: 故曰『臣弒君，子弒父，非一旦一夕之故也，其漸久矣』。

translations.literal: Therefore it is said: 'When a minister assassinates his ruler, or a son his father, it is not the work of a single morning or evening — its roots have been growing for a long time.'
translations.idiomatic: And so it is said: 'When a minister assassinates his ruler, or a son his father, it is not the work of a single day — its roots have been growing for a long time.'

sentences.zh: 故有國者不可以不知春秋，前有讒而弗見，後有賊而不知。

translations.literal: Therefore one who possesses a state must not fail to know the Spring and Autumn Annals, lest slander approach from the front unseen, and treachery from behind unrecognized.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore a ruler must not fail to know the Spring and Autumn Annals, lest slander approach unseen from the front and treachery creep up unrecognized from behind.

sentences.zh: 為人臣者不可以不知春秋，守經事而不知其宜，遭變事而不知其權。

translations.literal: One who serves as a minister must not fail to know the Spring and Autumn Annals, lest in handling routine affairs he not know what is fitting, and in meeting crises he not know how to adapt.
translations.idiomatic: A minister must not fail to know the Spring and Autumn Annals, lest in managing routine affairs he not know what is fitting, and in facing crises he not know how to adapt.

sentences.zh: 為人君父而不通於春秋之義者，必蒙首惡之名。

translations.literal: A ruler or father who does not understand the principles of the Spring and Autumn Annals will inevitably bear the name of chief malefactor.
translations.idiomatic: A ruler or father who does not grasp the principles of the Spring and Autumn Annals will inevitably bear the stigma of chief malefactor.

sentences.zh: 為人臣子而不通於春秋之義者，必陷篡弒之誅，死罪之名。

translations.literal: A minister or son who does not understand the principles of the Spring and Autumn Annals will inevitably fall into the punishment for usurpation and regicide, and bear the name of a capital crime.
translations.idiomatic: A minister or son who does not grasp the principles of the Spring and Autumn Annals will inevitably fall into the punishment for usurpation and regicide, condemned in the name of a capital crime.

sentences.zh: 其實皆以為善，為之不知其義，被之空言而不敢辭。

translations.literal: In truth, all believe they are acting for the good, yet they act without understanding the principles behind their actions, and are bound by empty words they dare not reject.
translations.idiomatic: In truth, all believe they are acting for the good, yet they act without understanding the principles behind their deeds, bound by empty formulations they dare not reject.

sentences.zh: 夫不通禮義之旨，至於君不君，臣不臣，父不父，子不子。

translations.literal: When one does not understand the essence of ritual and righteousness, it comes to the point where the ruler is not a ruler, the minister not a minister, the father not a father, and the son not a son.
translations.idiomatic: Without understanding the essence of ritual and righteousness, matters reach the point where a ruler fails to be a ruler, a minister fails to be a minister, a father fails to be a father, and a son fails to be a son.

sentences.zh: 夫君不君則犯，臣不臣則誅，父不父則無道，子不子則不孝。

translations.literal: When the ruler fails as a ruler, his authority is violated; when the minister fails as a minister, he is punished; when the father fails as a father, he is without the Way; when the son fails as a son, he is unfilial.
translations.idiomatic: When a ruler fails as a ruler, his authority is violated. When a minister fails as a minister, he faces punishment. When a father fails as a father, he loses the Way. When a son fails as a son, he is unfilial.

sentences.zh: 此四行者，天下之大過也。

translations.literal: These four transgressions are the greatest offenses under heaven.
translations.idiomatic: These four transgressions are the gravest offenses under heaven.

sentences.zh: 以天下之大過予之，則受而弗敢辭。

translations.literal: When one is charged with the greatest offenses under heaven, one must accept the condemnation and dare not refuse.
translations.idiomatic: When charged with the greatest offenses under heaven, one must accept the condemnation and cannot refuse.

sentences.zh: 故春秋者，禮義之大宗也。

translations.literal: Therefore the Spring and Autumn Annals is the great foundation of ritual and righteousness.
translations.idiomatic: The Spring and Autumn Annals is therefore the great repository of ritual and righteousness.

sentences.zh: 夫禮禁未然之前，法施已然之後；

translations.literal: Now, ritual prohibits before an offense occurs; law punishes after it has occurred;
translations.idiomatic: Ritual prevents offenses before they occur; law punishes after the fact.

sentences.zh: 法之所為用者易見，而禮之所為禁者難知。」

translations.literal: what law accomplishes through enforcement is easy to see, but what ritual accomplishes through prohibition is hard to perceive.'
translations.idiomatic: The workings of law through enforcement are easy to see; the workings of ritual through prevention are difficult to perceive.'

sentences.zh: 壺遂曰：「孔子之時，上無明君，下不得任用，故作春秋，垂空文以斷禮義，當一王之法。

translations.literal: Hu Sui said: 'In the time of Confucius, above there was no enlightened sovereign, and below he was not given office. Therefore he composed the Spring and Autumn Annals, bequeathing his written judgments to determine matters of ritual and righteousness, serving as the code of a single king.
translations.idiomatic: Hu Sui replied: 'In Confucius's time, there was no enlightened sovereign above, and below he was denied office. And so he composed the Spring and Autumn Annals, bequeathing his written judgments on ritual and righteousness to serve as the code of a true king.

sentences.zh: 今夫子上遇明天子，下得守職，萬事既具，咸各序其宜，夫子所論，欲以何明？」

translations.literal: Now, sir, above you have encountered an enlightened Son of Heaven, and below you hold your office. All affairs are already in order, each arranged as it should be. What do you wish to illuminate through your discussion?'
translations.idiomatic: Now, sir, you serve an enlightened Son of Heaven above, and hold your office below. All affairs of state are already in order, each properly arranged. What, then, do you seek to illuminate through your work?'

sentences.zh: 太史公曰：「唯唯，否否，不然。

translations.literal: The Grand Historian said: 'Yes, yes — no, no — it is not like that.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Historian replied: 'Yes, yes — no, no. It is not as you say.

sentences.zh: 余聞之先人曰：『伏羲至純厚，作易八卦。

translations.literal: I heard my late father say: "Fuxi, of the utmost purity and generosity, created the eight trigrams of the Changes.
translations.idiomatic: I heard my late father say: "Fuxi, a man of the utmost purity and depth, created the eight trigrams of the Changes.

sentences.zh: 堯舜之盛，尚書載之，禮樂作焉。

translations.literal: The glory of Yao and Shun is recorded in the Documents, and from it rites and music were composed.
translations.idiomatic: The glory of Yao and Shun is set down in the Documents, and from their age rites and music arose.

sentences.zh: 湯武之隆，詩人歌之。

translations.literal: The greatness of Tang and Wu was celebrated in the songs of the poets.
translations.idiomatic: The greatness of Tang and Wu was celebrated in the songs of the poets.

sentences.zh: 春秋采善貶惡，推三代之德，褒周室，非獨刺譏而已也。』

translations.literal: The Spring and Autumn Annals selects what is good and censures what is evil, extends the virtue of the Three Dynasties, and praises the house of Zhou — it is not merely a work of criticism and satire."
translations.idiomatic: The Spring and Autumn Annals commends the good and condemns the evil, carries forward the virtue of the Three Dynasties, and extols the house of Zhou. It is not merely a work of criticism and satire."

sentences.zh: 漢興以來，至明天子，獲符瑞，封禪，改正朔，易服色，受命於穆清，澤流罔極，海外殊俗，重譯款塞，請來獻見者，不可勝道。

translations.literal: Since the rise of the Han, down to the present enlightened Son of Heaven, auspicious omens have been received, the feng and shan sacrifices performed, the calendar reformed, the ceremonial colors changed, and the Mandate received in solemn purity. The imperial bounty flows without limit. Peoples of exotic customs from beyond the seas, through layers of interpreters, knock at the frontier passes seeking to come and present tribute — they are too many to enumerate.
translations.idiomatic: Since the founding of the Han, down to the present enlightened Son of Heaven, auspicious omens have been received, the feng and shan sacrifices performed, the calendar reformed, and the ceremonial colors changed. The Mandate has been received in solemn purity, and the imperial bounty flows without limit. Peoples of exotic customs from beyond the seas, communicating through layers of interpreters, knock at the frontier passes seeking to present tribute — they are too many to count.

sentences.zh: 臣下百官力誦聖德，猶不能宣盡其意。

translations.literal: The ministers and hundred officials have labored to extol the sagely virtue, yet still cannot fully express its meaning.
translations.idiomatic: The ministers and officials have labored to extol the sovereign's sagely virtue, yet cannot fully convey its scope.

sentences.zh: 且士賢能而不用，有國者之恥；

translations.literal: Moreover, when worthy and capable men are not employed, it is a disgrace to the ruler of the state;
translations.idiomatic: Moreover, when worthy and capable men go unused, it is a disgrace to the ruler;

sentences.zh: 主上明聖而德不布聞，有司之過也。

translations.literal: when the sovereign is enlightened and sagely yet his virtue is not widely known, it is the fault of his officials.
translations.idiomatic: when the sovereign is enlightened and sagely yet his virtue goes unheralded, the fault lies with his officials.

sentences.zh: 且余嘗掌其官，廢明聖盛德不載，滅功臣世家賢大夫之業不述，墮先人所言，罪莫大焉。

translations.literal: Moreover, I have held this office; to let the enlightened and sagely sovereign's abundant virtue go unrecorded, to let the achievements of meritorious ministers, hereditary houses, and worthy grandees go undescribed, to let the words of my forebears fall to ruin — no crime could be greater.
translations.idiomatic: I myself have held this office. To let the brilliant virtue of the enlightened sovereign go unrecorded, to leave the achievements of meritorious ministers, great families, and worthy grandees undescribed, to let my forebears' charge fall into oblivion — no crime could be greater.

sentences.zh: 余所謂述故事，整齊其世傳，非所謂作也，而君比之於春秋，謬矣。」

translations.literal: What I call 'narrating past events and ordering the traditions handed down through the generations' is not what one calls 'composing.' For you to compare my work with the Spring and Autumn Annals is mistaken.'
translations.idiomatic: What I call 'narrating past events and putting the inherited traditions in order' is not what one calls 'composing' an original work. To compare it with the Spring and Autumn Annals is a mistake.'

sentences.zh: 於是論次其文。

translations.literal: Thereupon he set about arranging and ordering his writings.
translations.idiomatic: And so he set about arranging and ordering his writings.

sentences.zh: 七年而太史公遭李陵之禍，幽於縲紲。

translations.literal: Seven years later, the Grand Historian suffered the calamity of the Li Ling affair and was imprisoned in chains.
translations.idiomatic: Seven years later, the Grand Historian was caught up in the Li Ling affair and was thrown into prison in chains.

sentences.zh: 乃喟然而嘆曰：「是余之罪也夫！

translations.literal: He sighed deeply and lamented: 'This is my crime!
translations.idiomatic: He heaved a deep sigh and cried: 'This is my own doing!

sentences.zh: 是余之罪也夫！

translations.literal: This is my crime!
translations.idiomatic: This is my own doing!

sentences.zh: 身毀不用矣。」

translations.literal: My body is ruined and I am of no further use.'
translations.idiomatic: My body is ruined. I am of no further use.'

sentences.zh: 退而深惟曰：「夫詩書隱約者，欲遂其志之思也。

translations.literal: He withdrew and reflected deeply, saying: 'Those in the Odes and Documents who expressed themselves in veiled and restrained language did so because they wished to fulfill the aspirations of their hearts.
translations.idiomatic: He withdrew into deep reflection and said: 'The authors of the Odes and the Documents who expressed themselves in veiled and restrained language did so to fulfill the aspirations burning within them.

sentences.zh: 昔西伯拘羑里，演周易；

translations.literal: In ancient times, the Earl of the West was imprisoned at Youli and elaborated the Changes of Zhou;
translations.idiomatic: In ancient times, the Earl of the West was imprisoned at Youli, and there he elaborated the Changes of Zhou;

sentences.zh: 孔子戹陳蔡，作春秋；

translations.literal: Confucius was trapped between Chen and Cai and composed the Spring and Autumn Annals;
translations.idiomatic: Confucius was besieged between Chen and Cai, and composed the Spring and Autumn Annals;

sentences.zh: 屈原放逐，著離騷；

translations.literal: Qu Yuan was banished and wrote the Li Sao;
translations.idiomatic: Qu Yuan was driven into exile and wrote the Li Sao;

sentences.zh: 左丘失明，厥有國語；

translations.literal: Zuo Qiu lost his sight, and so there came the Discourses of the States;
translations.idiomatic: Zuo Qiu lost his sight, and from that loss came the Discourses of the States;

sentences.zh: 孫子臏腳，而論兵法；

translations.literal: Sun Bin had his feet amputated, yet he composed his treatise on the art of war;
translations.idiomatic: Sun Bin had his kneecaps removed, yet he composed his treatise on the art of war;

sentences.zh: 不韋遷蜀，世傳呂覽；

translations.literal: Lü Buwei was exiled to Shu, and the Lüshi Chunqiu has been handed down through the ages;
translations.idiomatic: Lü Buwei was exiled to Shu, and the Lüshi Chunqiu has been handed down through the ages;

sentences.zh: 韓非囚秦，說難、孤憤；

translations.literal: Han Fei was imprisoned in Qin, and wrote 'The Difficulty of Persuasion' and 'The Solitary Man's Indignation';
translations.idiomatic: Han Fei was imprisoned in Qin, and there he wrote 'The Difficulty of Persuasion' and 'The Solitary Man's Indignation';

sentences.zh: 詩三百篇，大抵賢聖發憤之所為作也。

translations.literal: the three hundred poems of the Odes were, for the most part, composed by sages and worthy men pouring out their anguish.
translations.idiomatic: the three hundred poems of the Odes were, for the most part, works born of the anguish of sages and worthy men.

sentences.zh: 此人皆意有所郁結，不得通其道也，故述往事，思來者。」

translations.literal: All of these men had thoughts that were pent up within them, unable to find expression through their intended path. And so they narrated the events of the past, with their thoughts directed toward those who would come after.'
translations.idiomatic: Every one of these men had thoughts pent up within, unable to find expression through their intended path. And so they narrated the events of the past, with their thoughts directed toward future generations.'

sentences.zh: 於是卒述陶唐以來，至于麟止，自黃帝始。

translations.literal: Thereupon he completed his narrative from the time of Tao and Tang onward, down to the capture of the unicorn, beginning with the Yellow Emperor.
translations.idiomatic: And so at last he completed his narrative from the age of Yao onward, down to the capture of the unicorn, beginning with the Yellow Emperor.

sentences.zh: 維昔黃帝，法天則地，四聖遵序，各成法度；

translations.literal: In ancient times the Yellow Emperor took heaven as his model and earth as his pattern; the four sages followed in succession, each establishing his own institutions;
translations.idiomatic: In ancient times the Yellow Emperor modeled himself on heaven and patterned himself on earth. The four sages who followed each established institutions of his own;

sentences.zh: 唐堯遜位，虞舜不台；

translations.literal: Tang Yao abdicated, and Yu Shun did not presume;
translations.idiomatic: Yao of Tang abdicated the throne, and Shun of Yu did not presume to claim it lightly;

sentences.zh: 厥美帝功，萬世載之。

translations.literal: the beauty of their imperial achievements has been recorded for ten thousand generations.
translations.idiomatic: the splendor of their imperial achievements has been recorded for ten thousand generations.

sentences.zh: 作五帝本紀第一。

translations.literal: I composed the Annals of the Five Emperors, chapter one.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Annals of the Five Emperors as the first chapter.

sentences.zh: 維禹之功，九州攸同，光唐虞際，德流苗裔；

translations.literal: As for the achievements of Yu: the Nine Provinces were brought together, the glory of Tang and Yu was illuminated, and his virtue flowed down to his descendants;
translations.idiomatic: Through Yu's achievements, the Nine Provinces were brought into harmony, the glory of the Yao and Shun era was illuminated, and his virtue flowed down through his descendants;

sentences.zh: 夏桀淫驕，乃放鳴條。

translations.literal: Jie of Xia was dissolute and arrogant, and was therefore banished at Mingtiao.
translations.idiomatic: Jie of Xia was dissolute and arrogant, and so he was driven out at Mingtiao.

sentences.zh: 作夏本紀第二。

translations.literal: I composed the Annals of Xia, chapter two.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Annals of Xia as the second chapter.

sentences.zh: 維契作商，爰及成湯；

translations.literal: Xie founded the Shang, extending down to Cheng Tang;
translations.idiomatic: Xie founded the line of Shang, a line extending down to Cheng Tang;

sentences.zh: 太甲居桐，德盛阿衡；

translations.literal: Tai Jia dwelt at Tong, and the virtue of the Grand Steward flourished;
translations.idiomatic: Tai Jia was sent to Tong, and the virtue of the Grand Steward Yi Yin flourished;

sentences.zh: 武丁得說，乃稱高宗；

translations.literal: Wu Ding found Fu Yue, and was thereupon honored as Gao Zong;
translations.idiomatic: Wu Ding found Fu Yue, and earned the posthumous title Gao Zong;

sentences.zh: 帝辛湛湎，諸侯不享。

translations.literal: Emperor Xin indulged in drunkenness, and the feudal lords withheld their tributes.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Xin sank into drunken excess, and the feudal lords ceased to offer tribute.

sentences.zh: 作殷本紀第三。

translations.literal: I composed the Annals of Yin, chapter three.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Annals of Yin as the third chapter.

sentences.zh: 維棄作稷，德盛西伯；

translations.literal: Qi became Lord of Millet, and the virtue of the Earl of the West flourished;
translations.idiomatic: Qi became Lord of Millet, and virtue reached its height in the Earl of the West;

sentences.zh: 武王牧野，實撫天下；

translations.literal: King Wu fought at Muye and truly brought peace to all under heaven;
translations.idiomatic: King Wu triumphed at Muye and brought peace to all under heaven;

sentences.zh: 幽厲昏亂，既喪酆鎬；

translations.literal: Kings You and Li brought chaos and confusion, and thereupon lost Feng and Hao;
translations.idiomatic: Kings You and Li brought darkness and disorder, and Feng and Hao were lost;

sentences.zh: 陵遲至赧；

translations.literal: the decline continued down to King Nan;
translations.idiomatic: the decline dragged on until King Nan;

sentences.zh: 洛邑不祀。

translations.literal: at Luoyi the sacrifices ceased.
translations.idiomatic: and at Luoyi the sacrifices ceased.

sentences.zh: 作周本紀第四。

translations.literal: I composed the Annals of Zhou, chapter four.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Annals of Zhou as the fourth chapter.

sentences.zh: 維秦之先，伯翳佐禹；

translations.literal: Among the ancestors of Qin, Bo Yi assisted Yu;
translations.idiomatic: The ancestors of Qin trace back to Bo Yi, who assisted Yu;

sentences.zh: 穆公思義，悼豪之旅；

translations.literal: Duke Mu aspired to righteousness, yet grieved over his expedition against Hao;
translations.idiomatic: Duke Mu aspired to righteousness, yet grieved over his disastrous campaign;

sentences.zh: 以人為殉，詩歌黃鳥；

translations.literal: he used men as burial sacrifices, and the poem 'Yellow Bird' was sung;
translations.idiomatic: he buried living men as sacrificial companions, and the poem 'Yellow Bird' was sung in lament;

sentences.zh: 昭襄業帝。

translations.literal: Kings Zhao and Xiang laid the groundwork for empire.
translations.idiomatic: Kings Zhao and Xiang laid the foundations for empire.

sentences.zh: 作秦本紀第五。

translations.literal: I composed the Annals of Qin, chapter five.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Annals of Qin as the fifth chapter.

sentences.zh: 始皇既立，并兼六國，銷鋒鑄鐻，維偃干革，尊號稱帝，矜武任力；

translations.literal: Once the First Emperor was established, he annexed the six states, melted down weapons to cast bell frames, laid aside shields and leather armor, adopted the exalted title of Emperor, and gloried in martial force and the exercise of power;
translations.idiomatic: Once the First Emperor was established, he annexed the six states, melted down weapons to cast bell frames, laid aside shields and armor, assumed the exalted title of Emperor, and gloried in martial force;

sentences.zh: 二世受運，子嬰降虜。

translations.literal: the Second Emperor inherited the throne, and Ziying surrendered as a captive.
translations.idiomatic: the Second Emperor inherited the dynasty's fate, and Ziying surrendered in captivity.

sentences.zh: 作始皇本紀第六。

translations.literal: I composed the Annals of the First Emperor, chapter six.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Annals of the First Emperor as the sixth chapter.

sentences.zh: 秦失其道，豪桀并擾；

translations.literal: When Qin lost the Way, the heroes and bold men rose together in tumult;
translations.idiomatic: When Qin lost the Way, heroes and bold men rose together in revolt;

sentences.zh: 項梁業之，子羽接之；

translations.literal: Xiang Liang began the enterprise, and Xiang Yu carried it forward;
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Liang laid the foundations, and Xiang Yu carried the enterprise forward;

sentences.zh: 殺慶救趙，諸侯立之；

translations.literal: he slew Qing and rescued Zhao, and the feudal lords elevated him;
translations.idiomatic: he slew the Qin commander, rescued Zhao, and the feudal lords elevated him;

sentences.zh: 誅嬰背懷，天下非之。

translations.literal: he executed Ziying and betrayed King Huai, and all under heaven condemned him.
translations.idiomatic: but he executed Ziying and betrayed King Huai, and all under heaven condemned him.

sentences.zh: 作項羽本紀第七。

translations.literal: I composed the Annals of Xiang Yu, chapter seven.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Annals of Xiang Yu as the seventh chapter.

sentences.zh: 子羽暴虐，漢行功德；

translations.literal: Xiang Yu was cruel and tyrannical, while the Han practiced meritorious virtue;
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Yu was cruel and tyrannical, while the founder of Han practiced virtue and merit;

sentences.zh: 憤發蜀漢，還定三秦；

translations.literal: in righteous anger he rose from Shu and Han, returned and pacified the Three Qin;
translations.idiomatic: rising in fury from Shu and Han, he returned and pacified the Three Qin;

sentences.zh: 誅籍業帝，天下惟寧，改制易俗。

translations.literal: he destroyed Xiang Ji and established the imperial enterprise; all under heaven was at peace, and institutions and customs were reformed.
translations.idiomatic: he destroyed Xiang Ji and established the empire. All under heaven was brought to peace, and institutions and customs were reformed.

sentences.zh: 作高祖本紀第八。

translations.literal: I composed the Annals of Gaozu, chapter eight.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Annals of Gaozu as the eighth chapter.

sentences.zh: 惠之早霣，諸呂不台；

translations.literal: Emperor Hui died young, and the Lü clan did not act with propriety;
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Hui died young, and the Lü clan overstepped all bounds;

sentences.zh: 崇彊祿、產，諸侯謀之；

translations.literal: they elevated and empowered Lü Lu and Lü Chan, and the feudal lords plotted against them;
translations.idiomatic: they elevated Lü Lu and Lü Chan to power, and the feudal lords plotted against them;

sentences.zh: 殺隱幽友，大臣洞疑，遂及宗禍。

translations.literal: they killed in secret and imprisoned allies; the great ministers were pierced with suspicion, and ruin came upon the entire clan.
translations.idiomatic: they murdered in secret and imprisoned allies. The great ministers were seized with suspicion, and calamity engulfed the entire clan.

sentences.zh: 作呂太后本紀第九。

translations.literal: I composed the Annals of Empress Dowager Lü, chapter nine.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Annals of Empress Dowager Lü as the ninth chapter.

sentences.zh: 漢既初興，繼嗣不明，迎王踐祚，天下歸心；

translations.literal: After the Han first arose, the succession was unclear; a king was welcomed and ascended the throne, and the hearts of all under heaven turned to him;
translations.idiomatic: After the Han first arose and the succession was uncertain, a king was welcomed to the throne, and all hearts under heaven turned toward him;

sentences.zh: 蠲除肉刑，開通關梁，廣恩博施，厥稱太宗。

translations.literal: he abolished mutilating punishments, opened the passes and bridges, extended grace broadly and bestowed bounty generously, and was thereby honored as Taizong.
translations.idiomatic: he abolished mutilating punishments, opened the passes and bridges, extended grace widely and bestowed bounty generously, earning the temple name Taizong.

sentences.zh: 作孝文本紀第十。

translations.literal: I composed the Annals of Emperor Wen, chapter ten.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Annals of Emperor Wen as the tenth chapter.

sentences.zh: 諸侯驕恣，吳首為亂，京師行誅，七國伏辜，天下翕然，大安殷富。

translations.literal: The feudal lords grew arrogant and unruly; Wu was the first to rebel. The capital dispatched punishment, and the Seven Kingdoms were brought to account. All under heaven settled into order, enjoying great peace and abundant prosperity.
translations.idiomatic: The feudal lords grew arrogant and defiant. Wu was the first to rebel. The capital struck back, and the Seven Kingdoms were brought to justice. The realm settled into profound peace and abundant prosperity.

sentences.zh: 作孝景本紀第十一。

translations.literal: I composed the Annals of Emperor Jing, chapter eleven.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Annals of Emperor Jing as the eleventh chapter.

sentences.zh: 漢興五世，隆在建元，外攘夷狄，內修法度，封禪，改正朔，易服色。

translations.literal: In the fifth generation since the Han arose, greatness peaked in the Jianyuan era: externally the barbarians were driven back, internally institutions and laws were perfected, the feng and shan sacrifices were performed, the calendar was reformed, and the ceremonial colors were changed.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth generation of the Han, greatness reached its peak in the Jianyuan era. Abroad, the barbarians were driven back; at home, laws and institutions were perfected. The feng and shan sacrifices were performed, the calendar reformed, and the ceremonial colors changed.

sentences.zh: 作今上本紀第十二。

translations.literal: I composed the Annals of the Present Emperor, chapter twelve.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Annals of the Present Emperor as the twelfth chapter.

sentences.zh: 維三代尚矣，年紀不可考，蓋取之譜牒舊聞，本于茲，於是略推，作三代世表第一。

translations.literal: The Three Dynasties are remote in antiquity, and their chronology cannot be verified. I drew upon genealogical records and old traditions, took these as my basis, and made rough calculations. I composed the Chronological Table of the Three Dynasties as the first table.
translations.idiomatic: The Three Dynasties lie so far back in antiquity that their chronology cannot be verified. Drawing upon genealogical records and old traditions as my basis, I made rough calculations and composed the Chronological Table of the Three Dynasties as the first table.

sentences.zh: 幽厲之後，周室衰微，諸侯專政，春秋有所不紀；

translations.literal: After Kings You and Li, the Zhou court declined, the feudal lords governed independently, and the Spring and Autumn Annals left certain matters unrecorded;
translations.idiomatic: After Kings You and Li, the Zhou court declined, the feudal lords governed independently, and the Spring and Autumn Annals left certain matters unrecorded;

sentences.zh: 而譜牒經略，五霸更盛衰，欲睹周世相先後之意，作十二諸侯年表第二。

translations.literal: yet the genealogical records and classical outlines, with the Five Hegemons rising and falling in turn — wishing to display the order and succession of the Zhou generations, I composed the Chronological Table of the Twelve Feudal Lords as the second table.
translations.idiomatic: yet the genealogies and annals, with the Five Hegemons rising and falling in succession — wishing to display the order and succession of the Zhou age, I composed the Chronological Table of the Twelve Feudal Lords as the second table.

sentences.zh: 春秋之後，陪臣秉政，彊國相王；

translations.literal: After the Spring and Autumn period, subordinate ministers seized the reins of government, and powerful states proclaimed one another kings;
translations.idiomatic: After the Spring and Autumn period, subordinate ministers seized the reins of power, and the great states proclaimed one another kings;

sentences.zh: 以至于秦，卒并諸夏，滅封地，擅其號。

translations.literal: until it reached Qin, which ultimately annexed all the Xia states, abolished the fiefs, and monopolized the imperial title.
translations.idiomatic: until at last Qin annexed all the Xia states, abolished the fiefs, and seized the imperial title for itself.

sentences.zh: 作六國年表第三。

translations.literal: I composed the Chronological Table of the Six States as the third table.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Chronological Table of the Six States as the third table.

sentences.zh: 秦既暴虐，楚人發難，項氏遂亂，漢乃扶義征伐；

translations.literal: After Qin became tyrannical, the Chu people rose in revolt, the Xiang clan brought turmoil, and the Han took up the cause of righteousness in its campaigns;
translations.idiomatic: After Qin descended into tyranny, the people of Chu rose in revolt, the Xiang clan brought turmoil, and the Han took up the banner of righteousness in its campaigns;

sentences.zh: 八年之閒，天下三嬗，事繁變眾，故詳著秦楚之際月表第四。

translations.literal: In the span of eight years, the realm changed hands three times, events were many and transformations numerous. Therefore I set down in detail the Monthly Table of the Qin-Chu Interregnum as the fourth table.
translations.idiomatic: In the span of eight years, the realm changed hands three times amid a profusion of events and transformations. And so I set down in detail the Monthly Table of the Qin-Chu Interregnum as the fourth table.

sentences.zh: 漢興已來，至于太初百年，諸侯廢立分削，譜紀不明，有司靡踵，彊弱之原云以世。

translations.literal: From the rise of the Han down to the Taichu era, a span of a hundred years, feudal lords were deposed and installed, divided and reduced; the genealogical records were unclear, the officials did not follow through, and the origins of strength and weakness shifted generation by generation.
translations.idiomatic: In the century from the rise of the Han to the Taichu era, feudal lords were deposed and installed, their domains divided and diminished. The genealogical records grew unclear, officials failed to maintain them, and the fortunes of the strong and weak shifted with each generation.

sentences.zh: 作漢興已來諸侯年表第五。

translations.literal: I composed the Chronological Table of the Feudal Lords since the Rise of Han as the fifth table.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Chronological Table of the Feudal Lords since the Rise of the Han as the fifth table.

sentences.zh: 維高祖元功，輔臣股肱，剖符而爵，澤流苗裔，忘其昭穆，或殺身隕國。

translations.literal: Regarding the original achievements of Gaozu: his assistant ministers were his arms and legs. They split tallies and received noble rank, and their bounty flowed to their descendants. Some forgot the order of their ancestral tablets; others lost their lives and their states.
translations.idiomatic: The original achievements of Gaozu depended on his assistant ministers, who were his very arms and legs. They split tallies to receive noble rank, and the imperial bounty flowed down to their descendants. Some forgot the order of their ancestral lineage; others lost their lives and their domains.

sentences.zh: 作高祖功臣侯者年表第六。

translations.literal: I composed the Chronological Table of Meritorious Ministers Ennobled by Gaozu as the sixth table.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Chronological Table of Meritorious Ministers Ennobled by Gaozu as the sixth table.

sentences.zh: 惠景之閒，維申功臣宗屬爵邑，作惠景閒侯者年表第七。

translations.literal: Between the reigns of Emperors Hui and Jing, additional meritorious ministers and imperial kinsmen were ennobled with ranks and fiefs. I composed the Chronological Table of Marquises between the Hui and Jing Eras as the seventh table.
translations.idiomatic: Between the reigns of Emperors Hui and Jing, additional meritorious ministers and imperial kinsmen were granted ranks and fiefs. And so I composed the Chronological Table of Marquises of the Hui-Jing Era as the seventh table.

sentences.zh: 北討彊胡，南誅勁越，征伐夷蠻，武功爰列。
