translations.literal: To the north the mighty Hu were attacked, to the south the powerful Yue were chastised, and the barbarian peoples of the four quarters were campaigned against — martial achievements were set forth in order.
translations.idiomatic: The mighty Hu were attacked in the north, the powerful Yue chastised in the south, and campaigns launched against the barbarian peoples on every frontier. Martial achievements were thus set forth in order.

sentences.zh: 作建元以來侯者年表第八。

translations.literal: I composed the Chronological Table of Marquises since the Jianyuan Era as the eighth table.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Chronological Table of Marquises since the Jianyuan Era as the eighth table.

sentences.zh: 諸侯既彊，七國為從，子弟眾多，無爵封邑，推恩行義，其埶銷弱，德歸京師。

translations.literal: When the feudal lords had grown powerful and the Seven Kingdoms conspired in alliance, the princes' sons and brothers were many but without ranks or fiefs. The policy of 'extending grace' was implemented in the name of righteousness, their power was gradually weakened, and authority reverted to the capital.
translations.idiomatic: When the feudal lords had grown powerful and the Seven Kingdoms conspired together, the princes had many sons and brothers but no ranks or fiefs to give them. The 'extending grace' policy was enacted in the name of righteousness, gradually weakening the lords' power and returning authority to the capital.

sentences.zh: 作王子侯者年表第九。

translations.literal: I composed the Chronological Table of Royal Sons Ennobled as Marquises as the ninth table.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Chronological Table of Royal Sons Ennobled as Marquises as the ninth table.

sentences.zh: 國有賢相良將，民之師表也。

translations.literal: When a state has worthy chancellors and able generals, they serve as models and exemplars for the people.
translations.idiomatic: When a state has worthy chancellors and able generals, they serve as models and exemplars for the people.

sentences.zh: 維見漢興以來將相名臣年表，賢者記其治，不賢者彰其事。

translations.literal: Examining the chronological table of generals, chancellors, and famous ministers since the rise of the Han: the worthy are recorded for their governance, and the unworthy have their deeds exposed.
translations.idiomatic: In examining the table of generals, chancellors, and famous ministers since the rise of the Han, the worthy are recorded for their good governance, and the unworthy have their deeds laid bare.

sentences.zh: 作漢興以來將相名臣年表第十。

translations.literal: I composed the Chronological Table of Generals, Chancellors, and Famous Ministers since the Rise of the Han as the tenth table.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Chronological Table of Generals, Chancellors, and Famous Ministers since the Rise of the Han as the tenth table.

sentences.zh: 維三代之禮，所損益各殊務，然要以近性情，通王道，故禮因人質為之節文，略協古今之變。

translations.literal: As for the rites of the Three Dynasties, what each subtracted and added differed according to circumstances, yet the essential aim was to accord with human nature and to realize the kingly Way. Therefore ritual adapts its forms and regulations to the substance of human nature, broadly harmonizing with the transformations of past and present.
translations.idiomatic: The rites of the Three Dynasties each added and subtracted differently according to circumstances, yet all sought to accord with human nature and realize the kingly Way. Ritual therefore adapts its forms and regulations to the substance of human nature, broadly harmonizing with the changes of past and present.

sentences.zh: 作禮書第一。

translations.literal: I composed the Treatise on Ritual as the first treatise.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Treatise on Ritual as the first treatise.

sentences.zh: 樂者，所以移風易俗也。

translations.literal: Music is the means by which customs and habits are transformed.
translations.idiomatic: Music is the means by which customs and habits are transformed.

sentences.zh: 自雅頌聲興，則已好鄭衛之音，鄭衛之音所從來久矣。

translations.literal: Since the sounds of the Ya and Song arose, people already preferred the music of Zheng and Wei; the music of Zheng and Wei has been around for a long time.
translations.idiomatic: Even after the elegant Ya and Song odes arose, people still preferred the music of Zheng and Wei — and that music has a long history.

sentences.zh: 人情之所感，遠俗則懷。

translations.literal: What stirs human feeling — when customs are distant, one yearns for home.
translations.idiomatic: What stirs human feeling is this: the farther one is from home customs, the deeper the longing.

sentences.zh: 比樂書以述來古，作樂書第二。

translations.literal: Comparing and arranging the treatise on music to narrate the transmission from antiquity, I composed the Treatise on Music as the second treatise.
translations.idiomatic: Arranging the sources on music to narrate its transmission from antiquity, I composed the Treatise on Music as the second treatise.

sentences.zh: 非兵不彊，非德不昌，黃帝、湯、武以興，桀、紂、二世以崩，可不慎歟？

translations.literal: Without military force, a state cannot be strong; without virtue, it cannot flourish. The Yellow Emperor, Tang, and Wu rose through both; Jie, Zhou, and the Second Emperor fell because they lacked them. Can one not be cautious?
translations.idiomatic: Without military force, no state can be strong; without virtue, none can flourish. The Yellow Emperor, Tang, and Wu rose through both, while Jie, Zhou, and the Second Emperor fell for want of them. Must one not exercise the utmost caution?

sentences.zh: 司馬法所從來尚矣，太公、孫、吳、王子能紹而明之，切近世，極人變。

translations.literal: The Sima Methods of War have an ancient origin. The Grand Duke, Sunzi, Wu Qi, and Prince Cheng were able to carry them forward and illuminate them, making them relevant to the modern world and exhausting the range of human adaptation.
translations.idiomatic: The Sima Methods of War have ancient origins. The Grand Duke, Sunzi, Wu Qi, and Prince Cheng carried them forward and elucidated them, making them relevant to the modern age and exploring the full range of human adaptation.

sentences.zh: 作律書第三。

translations.literal: I composed the Treatise on the Pitch-Pipes as the third treatise.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Treatise on the Pitch-Pipes as the third treatise.

sentences.zh: 律居陰而治陽，歷居陽而治陰，律歷更相治，閒不容翲忽。

translations.literal: The pitch-pipes reside in yin and govern yang; the calendar resides in yang and governs yin. Pitch-pipes and calendar alternately govern one another, with no room for the slightest error.
translations.idiomatic: The pitch-pipes reside in yin yet govern yang; the calendar resides in yang yet governs yin. The two govern each other in turn, admitting not the slightest discrepancy.

sentences.zh: 五家之文怫異，維太初之元論。

translations.literal: The writings of the five schools were contradictory; only the Taichu reform's fundamental theory prevails.
translations.idiomatic: The writings of the five schools were contradictory and confused. Only the fundamental principles of the Taichu reform prevail.

sentences.zh: 作歷書第四。

translations.literal: I composed the Treatise on the Calendar as the fourth treatise.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Treatise on the Calendar as the fourth treatise.

sentences.zh: 星氣之書，多雜禨祥，不經；

translations.literal: The writings on stars and vapors are largely mixed with omens and portents, and are uncanonical;
translations.idiomatic: The writings on stars and vapors are largely mixed up with omens and portents, straying from canonical authority;

sentences.zh: 推其文，考其應，不殊。

translations.literal: yet when one traces their texts and examines their correspondences, they do not differ.
translations.idiomatic: yet when one traces their texts and examines their correspondences, they prove consistent.

sentences.zh: 比集論其行事，驗于軌度以次，作天官書第五。

translations.literal: Compiling and arranging discussions of their practical application, verifying them against orbital measurements and ordering them, I composed the Treatise on the Celestial Offices as the fifth treatise.
translations.idiomatic: Compiling discussions of their practical application, verifying them against orbital measurements, and arranging them in order, I composed the Treatise on the Celestial Offices as the fifth treatise.

sentences.zh: 受命而王，封禪之符罕用，用則萬靈罔不禋祀。

translations.literal: When a ruler receives the Mandate and becomes king, the rites of feng and shan are rarely performed; when they are, all ten thousand spirits receive solemn sacrifice.
translations.idiomatic: When a ruler receives the Mandate and becomes king, the feng and shan sacrifices are performed only on the rarest occasions; yet when they are, every spirit receives solemn worship.

sentences.zh: 追本諸神名山大川禮，作封禪書第六。

translations.literal: Tracing the origins of the rites for the various spirits, famous mountains, and great rivers, I composed the Treatise on the Feng and Shan Sacrifices as the sixth treatise.
translations.idiomatic: Tracing the origins of the rites for the spirits, the famous mountains, and the great rivers, I composed the Treatise on the Feng and Shan Sacrifices as the sixth treatise.

sentences.zh: 維禹浚川，九州攸寧；

translations.literal: Yu dredged the rivers, and the Nine Provinces were brought to peace;
translations.idiomatic: Yu dredged the rivers, and the Nine Provinces were brought to peace;

sentences.zh: 爰及宣防，決瀆通溝。

translations.literal: extending to the work of declaring flood defenses, breaching channels, and connecting waterways.
translations.idiomatic: extending to the work of flood prevention — breaching channels and connecting waterways.

sentences.zh: 作河渠書第七。

translations.literal: I composed the Treatise on Rivers and Canals as the seventh treatise.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Treatise on Rivers and Canals as the seventh treatise.

sentences.zh: 維幣之行，以通農商；

translations.literal: The circulation of currency serves to connect agriculture and commerce;
translations.idiomatic: Currency circulates in order to connect agriculture and commerce;

sentences.zh: 其極則玩巧，并兼茲殖，爭於機利，去本趨末。

translations.literal: at its extreme, people become obsessed with cleverness, merge and absorb to multiply their wealth, contend for opportunistic profit, and abandon the root to chase secondary pursuits.
translations.idiomatic: taken to extremes, people grow obsessed with cleverness, swallow up rivals to multiply their wealth, compete for opportunistic profit, and abandon the fundamental to chase the secondary.

sentences.zh: 作平準書以觀事變，第八。

translations.literal: I composed the Treatise on the Balanced Standard to observe the changes of affairs, as the eighth treatise.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Treatise on the Balanced Standard to observe the unfolding of affairs, as the eighth treatise.

sentences.zh: 太伯避歷，江蠻是適；

translations.literal: Taibo yielded the succession and went to dwell among the Jiang barbarians;
translations.idiomatic: Taibo yielded the succession and went to live among the barbarians south of the Yangtze;

sentences.zh: 文武攸興，古公王跡。

translations.literal: the rise of Kings Wen and Wu traces back to the legacy of Gugong.
translations.idiomatic: the rise of Kings Wen and Wu traces back to the legacy of Gugong Danfu.

sentences.zh: 闔廬弒僚，賓服荊楚；

translations.literal: Helü assassinated Liao and brought Jing-Chu into submission;
translations.idiomatic: Helü assassinated Liao and forced Chu into submission;

sentences.zh: 夫差克齊，子胥鴟夷；

translations.literal: Fuchai defeated Qi, and Wu Zixu's body was cast into a leather sack;
translations.idiomatic: Fuchai triumphed over Qi, yet Wu Zixu was put in a leather sack and thrown into the river;

sentences.zh: 信嚭親越，吳國既滅。

translations.literal: Bo Pi was trusted though he sided with Yue, and the state of Wu was thereupon destroyed.
translations.idiomatic: Bo Pi was trusted despite his treacherous closeness to Yue, and the state of Wu was destroyed.

sentences.zh: 嘉伯之讓，作吳世家第一。

translations.literal: Admiring Taibo's act of yielding, I composed the Hereditary House of Wu as the first hereditary house.
translations.idiomatic: In admiration of Taibo's act of yielding, I composed the Hereditary House of Wu as the first hereditary house.

sentences.zh: 申、呂肖矣，尚父側微，卒歸西伯，文武是師；

translations.literal: The clans of Shen and Lü had declined; the Revered Father was lowly and obscure, yet in the end he found his way to the Earl of the West, and became the teacher of Wen and Wu;
translations.idiomatic: The clans of Shen and Lü had long since declined. The Revered Father dwelt in obscurity, yet in the end he found his way to the Earl of the West and became the teacher of Kings Wen and Wu;

sentences.zh: 功冠群公，繆權于幽；

translations.literal: his merit surpassed all the lords, and he exercised his authority shrewdly in the shadows;
translations.idiomatic: his merit crowned that of all the lords, and he wielded his authority shrewdly from the shadows;

sentences.zh: 番番黃髪，爰饗營丘。

translations.literal: with his distinguished white hair, he was rewarded with Yingqiu.
translations.idiomatic: in his distinguished old age, he was granted Yingqiu as his domain.

sentences.zh: 不背柯盟，桓公以昌，九合諸侯，霸功顯彰。

translations.literal: He did not violate the covenant at Ke; Duke Huan thereby flourished, convened the feudal lords nine times, and his hegemonic achievements shone brilliantly.
translations.idiomatic: He honored the covenant at Ke, and Duke Huan prospered. He convened the feudal lords nine times, and his hegemonic glory shone for all to see.

sentences.zh: 田闞爭寵，姜姓解亡。

translations.literal: The Tian and Kan clans competed for favor, and the Jiang surname was dissolved and perished.
translations.idiomatic: The Tian and Kan clans vied for power, and the house of Jiang was destroyed.

sentences.zh: 嘉父之謀，作齊太公世家第二。

translations.literal: Admiring the Revered Father's stratagems, I composed the Hereditary House of the Grand Duke of Qi as the second hereditary house.
translations.idiomatic: In admiration of the Grand Duke's stratagems, I composed the Hereditary House of the Grand Duke of Qi as the second hereditary house.

sentences.zh: 依之違之，周公綏之；

translations.literal: Whether they obeyed or defied, the Duke of Zhou brought them to peace;
translations.idiomatic: Whether they obeyed or defied, the Duke of Zhou brought them to peace;

sentences.zh: 憤發文德，天下和之；

translations.literal: he rose with passionate cultivation of civil virtue, and all under heaven was harmonized;
translations.idiomatic: rising with passionate determination, he cultivated civil virtue, and all under heaven was brought into harmony;

sentences.zh: 輔翼成王，諸侯宗周。

translations.literal: he assisted and sheltered King Cheng, and the feudal lords acknowledged Zhou as their sovereign.
translations.idiomatic: he sheltered and guided King Cheng, and the feudal lords acknowledged Zhou as their sovereign.

sentences.zh: 隱桓之際，是獨何哉？

translations.literal: Between the reigns of Dukes Yin and Huan — what was the cause of Lu's troubles?
translations.idiomatic: In the time of Dukes Yin and Huan — what brought about Lu's troubles?

sentences.zh: 三桓爭彊，魯乃不昌。

translations.literal: The Three Huan contended for power, and Lu therefore did not prosper.
translations.idiomatic: The Three Huan vied for dominance, and Lu ceased to prosper.

sentences.zh: 嘉旦金縢，作周公世家第三。

translations.literal: Admiring the Duke of Zhou's 'Metal-Bound Coffer,' I composed the Hereditary House of the Duke of Zhou as the third hereditary house.
translations.idiomatic: In admiration of the Duke of Zhou and the Metal-Bound Coffer, I composed the Hereditary House of the Duke of Zhou as the third hereditary house.

sentences.zh: 武王克紂，天下未協而崩。

translations.literal: King Wu conquered Zhou of Shang, but the realm had not yet been consolidated when he died.
translations.idiomatic: King Wu conquered Zhou of Shang, but died before the realm was fully consolidated.

sentences.zh: 成王既幼，管蔡疑之，淮夷叛之，於是召公率德，安集王室，以寧東土。

translations.literal: King Cheng was young; Guan and Cai harbored suspicions, and the Huai Yi rebelled. Thereupon the Duke of Shao led with virtue, stabilized the royal house, and pacified the eastern lands.
translations.idiomatic: King Cheng was still a child. Guan and Cai harbored suspicions, and the Huai Yi rebelled. The Duke of Shao then led with virtue, stabilized the royal house, and pacified the eastern territories.

sentences.zh: 燕（易）［噲］之禪，乃成禍亂。

translations.literal: King Kuai of Yan's abdication brought about calamity and turmoil.
translations.idiomatic: King Kuai of Yan's ill-conceived abdication brought calamity and turmoil.

sentences.zh: 嘉甘棠之詩，作燕世家第四。

translations.literal: Admiring the 'Sweet Pear' poem, I composed the Hereditary House of Yan as the fourth hereditary house.
translations.idiomatic: In admiration of the poem 'Sweet Pear,' I composed the Hereditary House of Yan as the fourth hereditary house.

sentences.zh: 管蔡相武庚，將寧舊商；

translations.literal: Guan and Cai assisted Wu Geng, hoping to pacify the former Shang;
translations.idiomatic: Guan and Cai assisted Wu Geng, hoping to bring peace to the former Shang territories;

sentences.zh: 及旦攝政，二叔不饗；

translations.literal: but when the Duke of Zhou took the regency, the two uncles refused to submit;
translations.idiomatic: but when the Duke of Zhou assumed the regency, the two uncles refused to submit;

sentences.zh: 殺鮮放度，周公為盟；

translations.literal: Xian was killed and Du was banished; the Duke of Zhou established the covenant;
translations.idiomatic: Guan Shu was killed and Cai Shu exiled; the Duke of Zhou restored order through his covenant;

sentences.zh: 大任十子，周以宗彊。

translations.literal: Tai Ren bore ten sons, and Zhou thereby grew strong through its clan.
translations.idiomatic: Tai Ren bore ten sons, and through this great clan the house of Zhou grew powerful.

sentences.zh: 嘉仲悔過，作管蔡世家第五。

translations.literal: Admiring Zhong's repentance, I composed the Hereditary House of Guan and Cai as the fifth hereditary house.
translations.idiomatic: In admiration of Cai Zhong's repentance, I composed the Hereditary House of Guan and Cai as the fifth hereditary house.

sentences.zh: 王后不絕，舜禹是說；

translations.literal: The royal line was never severed — this is what Shun and Yu proclaimed;
translations.idiomatic: The royal line was never broken — as Shun and Yu themselves affirmed;

sentences.zh: 維德休明，苗裔蒙烈。

translations.literal: when virtue was splendid and radiant, their descendants were bathed in glory.
translations.idiomatic: when virtue shone splendidly, their descendants basked in its glory.

sentences.zh: 百世享祀，爰周陳杞，楚實滅之。

translations.literal: For a hundred generations they received sacrificial offerings — thus through Zhou down to Chen and Qi, until Chu finally destroyed them.
translations.idiomatic: For a hundred generations they received sacrificial offerings — through the Zhou down to the states of Chen and Qi, until Chu finally destroyed them.

sentences.zh: 齊田既起，舜何人哉？

translations.literal: After the Tian clan of Qi arose — Shun, what manner of man was he?
translations.idiomatic: And after the Tian clan of Qi arose — what manner of man, in the end, was Shun?

sentences.zh: 作陳杞世家第六。

translations.literal: I composed the Hereditary House of Chen and Qi as the sixth hereditary house.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Hereditary House of Chen and Qi as the sixth hereditary house.

sentences.zh: 收殷餘民，叔封始邑，申以商亂，酒材是告，及朔之生，衛頃不寧；

translations.literal: The remnant people of Yin were gathered, and Shu Feng founded the first settlement. The admonition cited the disorders of the Shang and warned against drink. When Shuo was born, Wei Qing was not at peace;
translations.idiomatic: The remnant population of Yin was gathered, and the uncle Kang Shu founded the first settlement. The proclamation cited the disorders of the Shang and warned against drink. When Shuo was born, the state of Wei was thrown into turmoil;

sentences.zh: 南子惡蒯聵，子父易名。

translations.literal: Nanzi detested Kuai Kui, and son and father exchanged their rightful names.
translations.idiomatic: Nanzi detested Kuai Kui, and father and son found their roles reversed.

sentences.zh: 周德卑微，戰國既彊，衛以小弱，角獨後亡。

translations.literal: When the virtue of Zhou had sunk low and the Warring States had grown powerful, Wei, though small and weak, was alone in being the last to perish.
translations.idiomatic: When Zhou's virtue had waned and the Warring States had risen to power, Wei, small and weak though it was, was the very last to perish.

sentences.zh: 喜彼康誥，作衛世家第七。

translations.literal: Delighting in the 'Announcement to Kang,' I composed the Hereditary House of Wei as the seventh hereditary house.
translations.idiomatic: Moved by the 'Announcement to Kang,' I composed the Hereditary House of Wei as the seventh hereditary house.

sentences.zh: 嗟箕子乎！

translations.literal: Alas for Jizi!
translations.idiomatic: Alas for Jizi!

sentences.zh: 嗟箕子乎！

translations.literal: Alas for Jizi!
translations.idiomatic: Alas, Jizi!

sentences.zh: 正言不用，乃反為奴。

translations.literal: His upright words went unheeded, and he was reduced to slavery.
translations.idiomatic: His honest counsel was rejected, and he was reduced to a slave.

sentences.zh: 武庚既死，周封微子。

translations.literal: After Wu Geng died, the Zhou enfeoffed Weizi.
translations.idiomatic: After Wu Geng's death, the Zhou enfeoffed Weizi.

sentences.zh: 襄公傷於泓，君子孰稱。

translations.literal: Duke Xiang was wounded at the battle of Hong, and gentlemen praised him for his conduct.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Xiang was wounded at the Hong River battle, and gentlemen praised his chivalry.

sentences.zh: 景公謙德，熒惑退行。

translations.literal: Duke Jing practiced humble virtue, and the planet Mars moved backward in its course.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Jing practiced humble virtue, and the planet Mars retreated in its course.

sentences.zh: 剔成暴虐，宋乃滅亡。

translations.literal: Ti Cheng was cruel and tyrannical, and Song thereupon perished.
translations.idiomatic: Ti Cheng was cruel and tyrannical, and the state of Song perished.

sentences.zh: 喜微子問太師，作宋世家第八。

translations.literal: Delighting in Weizi's inquiry of the Grand Tutor, I composed the Hereditary House of Song as the eighth hereditary house.
translations.idiomatic: Moved by Weizi's appeal to the Grand Tutor, I composed the Hereditary House of Song as the eighth hereditary house.

sentences.zh: 武王既崩，叔虞邑唐。

translations.literal: After King Wu died, Shu Yu was granted the settlement of Tang.
translations.idiomatic: After King Wu's death, Shu Yu was granted the fief of Tang.

sentences.zh: 君子譏名，卒滅武公。

translations.literal: The gentleman criticized the naming, and Duke Wu was ultimately overthrown.
translations.idiomatic: The gentleman criticized the ill-chosen name, and ultimately the line of Duke Wu was extinguished.

sentences.zh: 驪姬之愛，亂者五世；

translations.literal: The love for Lady Li brought chaos lasting five generations;
translations.idiomatic: The infatuation with Lady Li brought five generations of turmoil;

sentences.zh: 重耳不得意，乃能成霸。

translations.literal: Chong'er could not attain his ambitions, yet thereby achieved hegemony.
translations.idiomatic: Chong'er was denied his rightful place, and from that denial achieved hegemony.

sentences.zh: 六卿專權，晉國以秏。

translations.literal: The Six Ministers monopolized power, and the state of Jin was diminished.
translations.idiomatic: The Six Ministers seized power, and the state of Jin was drained away.

sentences.zh: 嘉文公錫珪鬯，作晉世家第九。

translations.literal: Admiring Duke Wen's bestowal of the jade scepter and libation wine, I composed the Hereditary House of Jin as the ninth hereditary house.
translations.idiomatic: In admiration of Duke Wen, who was bestowed the jade scepter and sacrificial wine, I composed the Hereditary House of Jin as the ninth hereditary house.

sentences.zh: 重黎業之，吳回接之；

translations.literal: Chongli began the enterprise, and Wu Hui carried it forward;
translations.idiomatic: Chongli began the enterprise, and Wu Hui carried it forward;

sentences.zh: 殷之季世，粥子牒之。

translations.literal: in the last days of the Yin, Yuxiong's sons continued the line.
translations.idiomatic: in the last days of the Yin, the line was continued through the sons of Yuxiong.

sentences.zh: 周用熊繹，熊渠是續。

translations.literal: The Zhou employed Xiong Yi, and Xiong Qu continued the line.
translations.idiomatic: Under the Zhou, Xiong Yi was enfeoffed, and Xiong Qu carried the line forward.

sentences.zh: 莊王之賢，乃復國陳；

translations.literal: Through the wisdom of King Zhuang, the state of Chen was restored;
translations.idiomatic: Through King Zhuang's virtue, the state of Chen was restored;

sentences.zh: 既赦鄭伯，班師華元。

translations.literal: Having pardoned the Earl of Zheng, he withdrew his troops, and Hua Yuan served as mediator.
translations.idiomatic: He pardoned the Earl of Zheng and withdrew his army; Hua Yuan served as mediator.

sentences.zh: 懷王客死，蘭咎屈原；

translations.literal: King Huai died abroad as a hostage, and Lan blamed Qu Yuan;
translations.idiomatic: King Huai died in captivity abroad, and Zilan placed the blame on Qu Yuan;

sentences.zh: 好諛信讒，楚并於秦。

translations.literal: by favoring flattery and trusting slander, Chu was absorbed by Qin.
translations.idiomatic: by favoring flattery and heeding slander, Chu was swallowed up by Qin.

sentences.zh: 嘉莊王之義，作楚世家第十。

translations.literal: Admiring King Zhuang's righteousness, I composed the Hereditary House of Chu as the tenth hereditary house.
translations.idiomatic: In admiration of King Zhuang's righteousness, I composed the Hereditary House of Chu as the tenth hereditary house.

sentences.zh: 少康之子，實賓南海，文身斷發，黿鱓與處，既守封禺，奉禹之祀。

translations.literal: The son of Shao Kang went as a guest to the southern seas, tattooing his body and cutting his hair short, living among turtles and crocodiles. He guarded Fengyu and maintained the sacrifices to Yu.
translations.idiomatic: The son of Shao Kang traveled to the southern seas, tattooing his body and cutting his hair, dwelling among turtles and crocodiles. He guarded the fief of Fengyu and maintained the sacrifices to Yu.

sentences.zh: 句踐困彼，乃用種、蠡。

translations.literal: When Goujian was trapped, he turned to Wen Zhong and Fan Li.
translations.idiomatic: When Goujian was besieged, he turned to Wen Zhong and Fan Li for aid.

sentences.zh: 嘉句踐夷蠻能修其德，滅彊吳以尊周室，作越王句踐世家第十一。

translations.literal: Admiring Goujian, a barbarian chieftain who was able to cultivate his virtue and destroy mighty Wu to honor the Zhou court, I composed the Hereditary House of King Goujian of Yue as the eleventh hereditary house.
translations.idiomatic: In admiration of Goujian — a barbarian chieftain who cultivated his virtue, destroyed mighty Wu, and honored the Zhou court — I composed the Hereditary House of King Goujian of Yue as the eleventh hereditary house.

sentences.zh: 桓公之東，太史是庸。

translations.literal: When Duke Huan moved east, the Grand Historian was his follower.
translations.idiomatic: When Duke Huan moved east, the Grand Historian was among his retinue.

sentences.zh: 及侵周禾，王人是議。

translations.literal: When Zheng seized the Zhou grain, the king's men debated the matter.
translations.idiomatic: When Zheng seized Zhou's grain, the king's men remonstrated.

sentences.zh: 祭仲要盟，鄭久不昌。

translations.literal: Zhai Zhong coerced an oath, and Zheng long failed to prosper.
translations.idiomatic: Zhai Zhong coerced an oath, and Zheng failed to prosper for a long time.

sentences.zh: 子產之仁，紹世稱賢。

translations.literal: The benevolence of Zi Chan earned him the praise of succeeding ages as a worthy man.
translations.idiomatic: The benevolence of Zi Chan has been praised by succeeding generations as the mark of a true worthy.

sentences.zh: 三晉侵伐，鄭納於韓。

translations.literal: The Three Jin invaded, and Zheng was absorbed into Han.
translations.idiomatic: The Three Jin states invaded, and Zheng was absorbed into Han.

sentences.zh: 嘉厲公納惠王，作鄭世家第十二。

translations.literal: Admiring Duke Li's reception of King Hui, I composed the Hereditary House of Zheng as the twelfth hereditary house.
translations.idiomatic: In admiration of Duke Li's reception of King Hui, I composed the Hereditary House of Zheng as the twelfth hereditary house.

sentences.zh: 維驥騄耳，乃章造父。

translations.literal: As for the steeds Ji and Lu'er — they made Zao Fu famous.
translations.idiomatic: The steeds Ji and Lu'er brought fame to Zao Fu.

sentences.zh: 趙夙事獻，衰續厥緒。

translations.literal: Zhao Su served Duke Xian, and Zhao Cui continued the line.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Su served Duke Xian, and Zhao Cui carried on the family's legacy.

sentences.zh: 佐文尊王，卒為晉輔。

translations.literal: He assisted Duke Wen in honoring the king, and ultimately became an aide of Jin.
translations.idiomatic: He aided Duke Wen in honoring the Zhou king and became a pillar of Jin.

sentences.zh: 襄子困辱，乃禽智伯。

translations.literal: Lord Xiang was beset and humiliated, yet ultimately captured Zhi Bo.
translations.idiomatic: Lord Xiang was beset and humiliated, yet in the end captured Zhi Bo.

sentences.zh: 主父生縛，餓死探爵。

translations.literal: The Master Father was captured alive and starved to death while searching for birds' eggs.
translations.idiomatic: The Master Father was seized alive and starved to death, reduced to searching for sparrows' eggs.

sentences.zh: 王遷辟淫，良將是斥。

translations.literal: King Qian was dissolute, and his able generals were dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: King Qian was dissolute and dismissed his ablest generals.

sentences.zh: 嘉鞅討周亂，作趙世家第十三。

translations.literal: Admiring Zhao Yang's suppression of the Zhou disorders, I composed the Hereditary House of Zhao as the thirteenth hereditary house.
translations.idiomatic: In admiration of Zhao Yang's suppression of disorder in the Zhou court, I composed the Hereditary House of Zhao as the thirteenth hereditary house.

sentences.zh: 畢萬爵魏，卜人知之。

translations.literal: Bi Wan was enfeoffed with Wei, and the diviners foresaw it.
translations.idiomatic: Bi Wan was granted the fief of Wei, as the diviners had foreseen.

sentences.zh: 及絳戮干，戎翟和之。

translations.literal: When the punishment at Jiang was carried out, the Rong and Di came to terms.
translations.idiomatic: When the sentence at Jiang was carried out, the Rong and Di were brought to terms.

sentences.zh: 文侯慕義，子夏師之。

translations.literal: Marquis Wen aspired to righteousness, and Zixia became his teacher.
translations.idiomatic: Marquis Wen aspired to righteousness and took Zixia as his teacher.

sentences.zh: 惠王自矜，齊秦攻之。

translations.literal: King Hui grew arrogant, and Qi and Qin attacked him.
translations.idiomatic: King Hui grew vainglorious, and Qi and Qin turned against him.

sentences.zh: 既疑信陵，諸侯罷之。

translations.literal: Once they suspected Lord Xinling, the feudal lords abandoned them.
translations.idiomatic: Once Lord Xinling fell under suspicion, the feudal lords withdrew their support.

sentences.zh: 卒亡大梁，王假廝之。

translations.literal: In the end Daliang was lost, and King Jia was captured there.
translations.idiomatic: In the end Daliang was lost, and King Jia was taken captive.

sentences.zh: 嘉武佐晉文申霸道，作魏世家第十四。

translations.literal: Admiring how Wu assisted Duke Wen of Jin in extending the way of hegemony, I composed the Hereditary House of Wei as the fourteenth hereditary house.
translations.idiomatic: In admiration of how Wu aided Duke Wen of Jin in forging the path of hegemony, I composed the Hereditary House of Wei as the fourteenth hereditary house.

sentences.zh: 韓厥陰德，趙武攸興。

translations.literal: Han Jue's hidden virtue enabled Zhao Wu to rise.
translations.idiomatic: The hidden virtue of Han Jue enabled Zhao Wu to flourish.

sentences.zh: 紹絕立廢，晉人宗之。

translations.literal: He continued what had been severed and established what had been abandoned, and the people of Jin honored him.
translations.idiomatic: He restored what had been severed and established what had been cast aside; the people of Jin honored him.

sentences.zh: 昭侯顯列，申子庸之。

translations.literal: Marquis Zhao distinguished himself, and Shen Buhai served as his minister.
translations.idiomatic: Marquis Zhao rose to prominence, with Shen Buhai serving as his chief minister.

sentences.zh: 疑非不信，秦人襲之。

translations.literal: Suspecting Han Fei yet not trusting him, the Qin attacked Han.
translations.idiomatic: The Qin doubted Han Fei and refused to trust him, then attacked the state of Han.

sentences.zh: 嘉厥輔晉匡周天子之賦，作韓世家第十五。

translations.literal: Admiring how Han aided Jin and rectified the levies of the Zhou Son of Heaven, I composed the Hereditary House of Han as the fifteenth hereditary house.
translations.idiomatic: In admiration of how Han aided Jin and rectified the tributes owed to the Zhou Son of Heaven, I composed the Hereditary House of Han as the fifteenth hereditary house.

sentences.zh: 完子避難，適齊為援，陰施五世，齊人歌之。

translations.literal: Wan's son fled trouble and went to Qi for refuge; the family quietly bestowed favors for five generations, and the people of Qi sang their praises.
translations.idiomatic: The son of Wan fled disaster and took refuge in Qi. The family bestowed quiet favors for five generations, and the people of Qi celebrated them in song.

sentences.zh: 成子得政，田和為侯。

translations.literal: Lord Cheng gained control of the government, and Tian He became a marquis.
translations.idiomatic: Lord Cheng seized the reins of government, and Tian He became a marquis.

sentences.zh: 王建動心，乃遷于共。

translations.literal: King Jian wavered in his resolve and was thereupon relocated to Gong.
translations.idiomatic: King Jian lost his nerve and was exiled to Gong.

sentences.zh: 嘉威、宣能撥濁世而獨宗周，作田敬仲完世家第十六。

translations.literal: Admiring how Kings Wei and Xuan were able to rise above a corrupt age and alone honor Zhou, I composed the Hereditary House of Tian Jing Zhongwan as the sixteenth hereditary house.
translations.idiomatic: In admiration of how Kings Wei and Xuan rose above a corrupt age to alone honor the Zhou court, I composed the Hereditary House of Tian Jing Zhongwan as the sixteenth hereditary house.

sentences.zh: 周室既衰，諸侯恣行。

translations.literal: After the Zhou court declined, the feudal lords acted without restraint.
translations.idiomatic: After the Zhou court declined, the feudal lords acted without restraint.

sentences.zh: 仲尼悼禮廢樂崩，追修經術，以達王道，匡亂世反之於正，見其文辭，為天下制儀法，垂六藝之統紀於後世。

translations.literal: Confucius mourned the abandonment of rites and the collapse of music, and pursued the restoration of the classical arts to realize the kingly Way, to set a disordered age right and return it to correctness. His writings and discourses established models and laws for all under heaven, and bequeathed the guiding principles of the Six Arts to posterity.
translations.idiomatic: Confucius mourned the abandonment of rites and the collapse of music. He restored the classical arts to realize the kingly Way, setting a disordered age aright and returning it to the correct path. Through his writings and discourses, he established models and standards for all under heaven, bequeathing the guiding principles of the Six Arts to posterity.

sentences.zh: 作孔子世家第十七。

translations.literal: I composed the Hereditary House of Confucius as the seventeenth hereditary house.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Hereditary House of Confucius as the seventeenth hereditary house.

sentences.zh: 桀、紂失其道而湯、武作，周失其道而春秋作。

translations.literal: When Jie and Zhou lost the Way, Tang and Wu arose; when the Zhou lost the Way, the Spring and Autumn Annals was composed.
translations.idiomatic: When Jie and Zhou lost the Way, Tang and Wu arose. When the Zhou lost the Way, the Spring and Autumn Annals was composed.

sentences.zh: 秦失其政，而陳涉發跡，諸侯作難，風起雲蒸，卒亡秦族。

translations.literal: When Qin lost its governance, Chen She rose up; the feudal lords rebelled, surging like wind and steaming like clouds, and ultimately destroyed the house of Qin.
translations.idiomatic: When Qin lost its grip on governance, Chen She rose up. The feudal lords rebelled, rising like a wind and surging like clouds, and ultimately destroyed the house of Qin.

sentences.zh: 天下之端，自涉發難。

translations.literal: The turning point of the realm began with Chen She raising the standard of revolt.
translations.idiomatic: The turning point for all under heaven began when Chen She raised the standard of revolt.

sentences.zh: 作陳涉世家第十八。

translations.literal: I composed the Hereditary House of Chen She as the eighteenth hereditary house.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Hereditary House of Chen She as the eighteenth hereditary house.

sentences.zh: 成皋之臺，薄氏始基。

translations.literal: At the terrace of Chenggao, the Bo clan first laid its foundation.
translations.idiomatic: At the terrace of Chenggao, the Bo clan first established its foundations.

sentences.zh: 詘意適代，厥崇諸竇。

translations.literal: Their ambitions humbled, they moved to Dai, and the various Dou branches rose to prominence.
translations.idiomatic: With their ambitions curbed, they moved to Dai, and the Dou branches rose to prominence.

sentences.zh: 栗姬偩貴，王氏乃遂。

translations.literal: Lady Li presumed upon her noble status, and the Wang clan thereupon rose to power.
translations.idiomatic: Lady Li presumed upon her noble position, and the Wang clan seized its chance to rise.

sentences.zh: 陳後太驕，卒尊子夫。

translations.literal: Empress Chen was too arrogant, and ultimately Lady Zifu was honored instead.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Chen grew too arrogant, and in the end Lady Wei Zifu was elevated in her place.

sentences.zh: 嘉夫德若斯，作外戚世家十九。

translations.literal: Admiring how virtue manifests in such ways, I composed the Hereditary House of Imperial Relatives by Marriage as the nineteenth hereditary house.
translations.idiomatic: Reflecting on how virtue manifests in such ways, I composed the Hereditary House of Imperial Relatives by Marriage as the nineteenth hereditary house.

sentences.zh: 漢既譎謀，禽信於陳；

translations.literal: After the Han employed cunning stratagems and captured Han Xin at Chen;
translations.idiomatic: After the Han used cunning stratagems to capture Han Xin at Chen;

sentences.zh: 越荊剽輕，乃封弟交為楚王，爰都彭城，以彊淮泗，為漢宗藩。

translations.literal: since the people of the former Yue and Jing regions were reckless and impetuous, he enfeoffed his younger brother Jiao as King of Chu, establishing the capital at Pengcheng to strengthen the Huai and Si region as a clan bastion for the Han.
translations.idiomatic: the people of the former Yue and Jing regions being reckless and impetuous, he enfeoffed his brother Jiao as King of Chu with his capital at Pengcheng, to strengthen the Huai-Si region as a clan bastion of the Han.

sentences.zh: 戊溺於邪，禮復紹之。

translations.literal: Wu was consumed by depravity, and Li restored and continued the line.
translations.idiomatic: Wu was consumed by depravity, but Li restored and continued the line.

sentences.zh: 嘉游輔祖，作楚元王世家二十。

translations.literal: Admiring You's service in assisting the founder, I composed the Hereditary House of King Yuan of Chu as the twentieth hereditary house.
translations.idiomatic: In admiration of Liu Jiao's service to the dynastic founder, I composed the Hereditary House of King Yuan of Chu as the twentieth hereditary house.

sentences.zh: 維祖師旅，劉賈是與；

translations.literal: In the founder's military campaigns, Liu Jia was his companion;
translations.idiomatic: In the founder's military campaigns, Liu Jia served at his side;

sentences.zh: 為布所襲，喪其荊、吳。

translations.literal: he was attacked by Ying Bu and lost the territories of Jing and Wu.
translations.idiomatic: attacked by Ying Bu, he lost the territories of Jing and Wu.

sentences.zh: 營陵激呂，乃王瑯邪；

translations.literal: The Marquis of Yingling incited the Lü faction, and was thereupon made King of Langya;
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Yingling provoked the Lü faction and was made King of Langya;

sentences.zh: 怵午信齊，往而不歸，遂西入關，遭立孝文，獲復王燕。

translations.literal: he was alarmed by Wu, trusted in Qi, went there and did not return; then he went west through the passes, happened to participate in the installation of Emperor Wen, and regained the kingship of Yan.
translations.idiomatic: alarmed by Wu and placing trust in Qi, he went there and did not return. Then he entered the passes to the west, took part in the enthronement of Emperor Wen, and regained the kingship of Yan.

sentences.zh: 天下未集，賈、澤以族，為漢藩輔。

translations.literal: While the realm was not yet settled, Jia and Ze, as clansmen, served as bulwarks and supports of the Han.
translations.idiomatic: While the realm remained unsettled, Jia and Ze, as members of the imperial clan, served as the Han's shield and support.

sentences.zh: 作荊燕世家第二十一。

translations.literal: I composed the Hereditary House of Jing and Yan as the twenty-first hereditary house.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Hereditary House of Jing and Yan as the twenty-first hereditary house.

sentences.zh: 天下已平，親屬既寡；

translations.literal: When the realm was already at peace and the close kinsmen were few;
translations.idiomatic: Once the realm was at peace and close kinsmen were few;

sentences.zh: 悼惠先壯，實鎮東土。

translations.literal: King Daohui was the first to grow strong and truly anchored the eastern territories.
translations.idiomatic: King Daohui was the first to grow powerful, truly anchoring the eastern territories.

sentences.zh: 哀王擅興，發怒諸呂，駟鈞暴戾，京師弗許。

translations.literal: King Ai took unauthorized action and roused the anger of the Lü clan; Si Jun was violent and cruel, and the capital would not permit it.
translations.idiomatic: King Ai acted without authorization, provoking the fury of the Lü clan. Si Jun was violent and cruel, and the capital would not tolerate it.

sentences.zh: 厲之內淫，禍成主父。

translations.literal: Li's secret debauchery brought ruin upon the Master Father.
translations.idiomatic: The secret debauchery of King Li brought calamity upon the Master Father.

sentences.zh: 嘉肥股肱，作齊悼惠王世家第二十二。

translations.literal: Admiring how Fei served as the strong arm, I composed the Hereditary House of King Daohui of Qi as the twenty-second hereditary house.
translations.idiomatic: In admiration of how Fei served as a strong arm of the dynasty, I composed the Hereditary House of King Daohui of Qi as the twenty-second hereditary house.

sentences.zh: 楚人圍我滎陽，相守三年；

translations.literal: The Chu forces besieged our position at Xingyang, and both sides held their ground for three years;
translations.idiomatic: The Chu forces besieged Xingyang, and both sides held their ground for three years;

sentences.zh: 蕭何填撫山西，推計踵兵，給糧食不絕，使百姓愛漢，不樂為楚。

translations.literal: Xiao He pacified and stabilized the region west of the mountains, managed accounts and dispatched reinforcements, supplied grain and provisions without interruption, and caused the common people to love the Han and be unwilling to serve Chu.
translations.idiomatic: Xiao He stabilized the region west of the mountains, managed logistics, dispatched reinforcements, and kept grain flowing without interruption — ensuring the people loved the Han and refused to serve Chu.

sentences.zh: 作蕭相國世家第二十三。

translations.literal: I composed the Hereditary House of Chancellor Xiao as the twenty-third hereditary house.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Hereditary House of Chancellor Xiao as the twenty-third hereditary house.

sentences.zh: 與信定魏，破趙拔齊，遂弱楚人。

translations.literal: Together with Han Xin, he pacified Wei, defeated Zhao, and took Qi, thereby weakening the Chu forces.
translations.idiomatic: Together with Han Xin, he pacified Wei, crushed Zhao, and conquered Qi, thereby weakening Chu.

sentences.zh: 續何相國，不變不革，黎庶攸寧。

translations.literal: Succeeding Xiao He as chancellor, he made no changes and no reforms, and the common people found peace.
translations.idiomatic: Succeeding Chancellor Xiao, he changed nothing and reformed nothing — and the common people found peace.

sentences.zh: 嘉參不伐功矜能，作曹相國世家第二十四。

translations.literal: Admiring how Cao Can did not boast of his achievements or flaunt his abilities, I composed the Hereditary House of Chancellor Cao as the twenty-fourth hereditary house.
translations.idiomatic: In admiration of Cao Can, who never boasted of his achievements or flaunted his abilities, I composed the Hereditary House of Chancellor Cao as the twenty-fourth hereditary house.

sentences.zh: 運籌帷幄之中，制勝於無形，子房計謀其事，無知名，無勇功，圖難於易，為大於細。

translations.literal: Devising strategies within the curtained tent, achieving victory through the intangible — Zifang planned his affairs with neither a reputation for knowledge nor fame for valor, overcoming difficulties through what appeared easy, accomplishing great things through what appeared small.
translations.idiomatic: Devising strategies within the curtained tent, winning victories through the intangible — Zhang Liang planned his campaigns with neither renown for knowledge nor fame for valor, overcoming the difficult through what seemed easy, and accomplishing the great through what seemed small.

sentences.zh: 作留侯世家第二十五。

translations.literal: I composed the Hereditary House of the Marquis of Liu as the twenty-fifth hereditary house.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Hereditary House of the Marquis of Liu as the twenty-fifth hereditary house.

sentences.zh: 六奇既用，諸侯賓從於漢；

translations.literal: After the Six Stratagems were put to use, the feudal lords submitted to the Han;
translations.idiomatic: Once the Six Stratagems were deployed, the feudal lords submitted to the Han;

sentences.zh: 呂氏之事，平為本謀，終安宗廟，定社稷。

translations.literal: in the Lü clan affair, Chen Ping was the original strategist, ultimately securing the ancestral temple and stabilizing the altars of state.
translations.idiomatic: in the Lü clan affair, Chen Ping was the mastermind, ultimately securing the ancestral temple and stabilizing the altars of state.

sentences.zh: 作陳丞相世家第二十六。

translations.literal: I composed the Hereditary House of Chancellor Chen as the twenty-sixth hereditary house.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Hereditary House of Chancellor Chen as the twenty-sixth hereditary house.

sentences.zh: 諸呂為從，謀弱京師，而勃反經合於權；

translations.literal: The Lü clan conspired together, plotting to weaken the capital, and Zhou Bo went against convention in accordance with the demands of the moment;
translations.idiomatic: The Lü clan conspired to weaken the capital, and Zhou Bo defied convention as the situation demanded;

sentences.zh: 吳楚之兵，亞夫駐於昌邑，以戹齊趙，而出委以梁。

translations.literal: During the Wu-Chu revolt, Ya Fu stationed himself at Changyi, constraining Qi and Zhao, and entrusted the front line to Liang.
translations.idiomatic: During the Wu-Chu revolt, Zhou Yafu stationed himself at Changyi, hemming in Qi and Zhao, and entrusted the front line to the kingdom of Liang.

sentences.zh: 作絳侯世家第二十七。

translations.literal: I composed the Hereditary House of the Marquis of Jiang as the twenty-seventh hereditary house.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Hereditary House of the Marquis of Jiang as the twenty-seventh hereditary house.

sentences.zh: 七國叛逆，蕃屏京師，唯梁為捍；

translations.literal: When the Seven Kingdoms rebelled, the only screen and defense for the capital was Liang;
translations.idiomatic: When the Seven Kingdoms rebelled, Liang alone served as the shield and defense of the capital;

sentences.zh: 偩愛矜功，幾獲于禍。

translations.literal: he presumed on imperial favor and boasted of his merits, nearly bringing disaster upon himself.
translations.idiomatic: yet he presumed upon the emperor's love and boasted of his merits, nearly bringing ruin upon himself.

sentences.zh: 嘉其能距吳楚，作梁孝王世家第二十八。

translations.literal: Admiring his ability to resist Wu and Chu, I composed the Hereditary House of King Xiao of Liang as the twenty-eighth hereditary house.
translations.idiomatic: In admiration of his ability to resist Wu and Chu, I composed the Hereditary House of King Xiao of Liang as the twenty-eighth hereditary house.

sentences.zh: 五宗既王，親屬洽和，諸侯大小為藩，爰得其宜，僭擬之事稍衰貶矣。

translations.literal: After the Five Lineages were established as kings, the kinship ties were harmonious, the feudal lords both great and small served as bulwarks, each finding its proper place, and acts of presumption and overreach gradually declined.
translations.idiomatic: Once the Five Lineages were established as kings, kinship ties were harmonized, the feudal lords great and small served as bulwarks, each finding its proper place, and acts of presumption gradually diminished.

sentences.zh: 作五宗世家第二十九。

translations.literal: I composed the Hereditary House of the Five Lineages as the twenty-ninth hereditary house.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Hereditary House of the Five Lineages as the twenty-ninth hereditary house.

sentences.zh: 三子之王，文辭可觀。

translations.literal: The kingship of the Three Sons — their literary compositions are worthy of admiration.
translations.idiomatic: The kingship of the Three Princes — their literary writings are worthy of admiration.

sentences.zh: 作三王世家第三十。

translations.literal: I composed the Hereditary House of the Three Kings as the thirtieth hereditary house.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Hereditary House of the Three Kings as the thirtieth hereditary house.

sentences.zh: 末世爭利，維彼奔義；

translations.literal: In a declining age when all competed for profit, they alone rushed toward righteousness;
translations.idiomatic: In a declining age when all competed for profit, they alone rushed toward righteousness;

sentences.zh: 讓國餓死，天下稱之。

translations.literal: they yielded their kingdom and starved to death, and all under heaven praised them.
translations.idiomatic: they yielded their kingdom and starved to death, and all under heaven praised them.

sentences.zh: 作伯夷列傳第一。

translations.literal: I composed the Biography of Bo Yi as the first biography.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Biography of Bo Yi as the first biography.

sentences.zh: 晏子儉矣，夷吾則奢；

translations.literal: Yanzi was frugal, while Guan Zhong was extravagant;
translations.idiomatic: Yanzi was frugal, while Guan Zhong was extravagant;

sentences.zh: 齊桓以霸，景公以治。

translations.literal: Duke Huan achieved hegemony through Guan Zhong, and Duke Jing achieved good governance through Yanzi.
translations.idiomatic: Through Guan Zhong, Duke Huan achieved hegemony; through Yanzi, Duke Jing achieved good governance.

sentences.zh: 作管晏列傳第二。

translations.literal: I composed the Biography of Guan and Yan as the second biography.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Biography of Guan Zhong and Yan Ying as the second biography.

sentences.zh: 李耳無為自化，清凈自正；

translations.literal: Li Er practiced non-action and let things transform of themselves, purity and stillness and self-correction;
translations.idiomatic: Li Er practiced non-action and let things transform of themselves, through purity and stillness achieving self-correction;

sentences.zh: 韓非揣事情，循埶理。

translations.literal: Han Fei fathomed the nature of affairs and followed the logic of power.
translations.idiomatic: Han Fei probed the nature of affairs and followed the logic of power.

sentences.zh: 作老子韓非列傳第三。

translations.literal: I composed the Biography of Laozi and Han Fei as the third biography.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Biography of Laozi and Han Fei as the third biography.

sentences.zh: 自古王者而有司馬法，穰苴能申明之。

translations.literal: From ancient times the kings had the Sima Methods of War, and Rangju was able to elucidate them.
translations.idiomatic: Since ancient times the kings had the Sima Methods of War, and Sima Rangju was able to elucidate them.

sentences.zh: 作司馬穰苴列傳第四。

translations.literal: I composed the Biography of Sima Rangju as the fourth biography.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Biography of Sima Rangju as the fourth biography.

sentences.zh: 非信廉仁勇不能傳兵論劍，與道同符，內可以治身，外可以應變，君子比德焉。

translations.literal: Without trustworthiness, integrity, benevolence, and courage, one cannot transmit the arts of warfare or discourse on swordsmanship. These accord with the Way: inwardly they can cultivate the person, outwardly they can adapt to change. The gentleman compares his virtue to them.
translations.idiomatic: Without trustworthiness, integrity, benevolence, and courage, no one can transmit the arts of war or discourse on swordsmanship. These accord with the Way itself: inwardly they cultivate the person, outwardly they enable adaptation. The gentleman measures his virtue against them.

sentences.zh: 作孫子吳起列傳第五。

translations.literal: I composed the Biography of Sunzi and Wu Qi as the fifth biography.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Biography of Sunzi and Wu Qi as the fifth biography.

sentences.zh: 維建遇讒，爰及子奢，尚既匡父，伍員奔吳。

translations.literal: Jian was slandered, and the calamity reached his father She. Shang tried to save his father, but Wu Yuan fled to Wu.
translations.idiomatic: Prince Jian fell victim to slander, and the disaster reached his father She. Shang tried to save their father, but Wu Yuan fled to Wu.

sentences.zh: 作伍子胥列傳第六。

translations.literal: I composed the Biography of Wu Zixu as the sixth biography.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Biography of Wu Zixu as the sixth biography.

sentences.zh: 孔氏述文，弟子興業，咸為師傅，崇仁厲義。

translations.literal: Confucius transmitted the literary heritage; his disciples carried on the enterprise, all becoming teachers and tutors, exalting benevolence and encouraging righteousness.
translations.idiomatic: Confucius transmitted the literary heritage, and his disciples carried on the enterprise. All became teachers and mentors, exalting benevolence and upholding righteousness.

sentences.zh: 作仲尼弟子列傳第七。

translations.literal: I composed the Biography of the Disciples of Confucius as the seventh biography.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Biography of the Disciples of Confucius as the seventh biography.

sentences.zh: 鞅去衛適秦，能明其術，彊霸孝公，後世遵其法。

translations.literal: Yang left Wei and went to Qin, where he was able to demonstrate his methods, making Duke Xiao strong enough to achieve hegemony. Later generations followed his laws.
translations.idiomatic: Shang Yang left Wei for Qin, where he demonstrated his methods, enabling Duke Xiao to achieve hegemony. Later generations followed his laws.

sentences.zh: 作商君列傳第八。

translations.literal: I composed the Biography of Lord Shang as the eighth biography.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Biography of Lord Shang as the eighth biography.

sentences.zh: 天下患衡秦毋饜，而蘇子能存諸侯，約從以抑貪彊。

translations.literal: All under heaven suffered from the insatiable horizontal alliance with Qin, yet Master Su was able to preserve the feudal lords, forging the vertical alliance to restrain the greedy and powerful.
translations.idiomatic: All under heaven suffered under Qin's insatiable horizontal alliance, yet Su Qin was able to preserve the feudal lords by forging the vertical alliance to check the greedy and powerful.

sentences.zh: 作蘇秦列傳第九。

translations.literal: I composed the Biography of Su Qin as the ninth biography.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Biography of Su Qin as the ninth biography.

sentences.zh: 六國既從親，而張儀能明其說，復散解諸侯。

translations.literal: After the Six States formed their vertical alliance, Zhang Yi was able to elucidate his arguments and once again scattered and dissolved the feudal lords' coalition.
translations.idiomatic: After the Six States formed their vertical alliance, Zhang Yi was able to deploy his persuasive arguments and once again scattered the feudal lords' coalition.

sentences.zh: 作張儀列傳第十。

translations.literal: I composed the Biography of Zhang Yi as the tenth biography.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Biography of Zhang Yi as the tenth biography.

sentences.zh: 秦所以東攘雄諸侯，樗裏、甘茂之策。

translations.literal: The reason Qin was able to push eastward and dominate the feudal lords lay in the strategies of Chuli and Gan Mao.
translations.idiomatic: Qin's ability to push eastward and dominate the feudal lords owed much to the strategies of Chuli Ji and Gan Mao.

sentences.zh: 作樗裏甘茂列傳第十一。

translations.literal: I composed the Biography of Chuli Ji and Gan Mao as the eleventh biography.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Biography of Chuli Ji and Gan Mao as the eleventh biography.

sentences.zh: 苞河山，圍大梁，使諸侯斂手而事秦者，魏冉之功。

translations.literal: To encompass the rivers and mountains, besiege Daliang, and make the feudal lords fold their hands and serve Qin — this was the achievement of Wei Ran.
translations.idiomatic: Encompassing the rivers and mountains, besieging Daliang, and compelling the feudal lords to fold their hands in submission to Qin — this was the achievement of Wei Ran.

sentences.zh: 作穰侯列傳第十二。

translations.literal: I composed the Biography of the Marquis of Rang as the twelfth biography.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Biography of the Marquis of Rang as the twelfth biography.

sentences.zh: 南拔鄢郢，北摧長平，遂圍邯鄲，武安為率；

translations.literal: To the south he conquered Yan and Ying, to the north he crushed Changping, and then besieged Handan — with Lord Wu'an in command;
translations.idiomatic: He conquered Yan and Ying in the south, crushed the army at Changping in the north, and then besieged Handan — all under Lord Wu'an's command;

sentences.zh: 破荊滅趙，王翦之計。

translations.literal: the destruction of Jing and the annihilation of Zhao were accomplished through Wang Jian's strategies.
translations.idiomatic: the destruction of Chu and the annihilation of Zhao were achieved through Wang Jian's strategies.

sentences.zh: 作白起王翦列傳第十三。

translations.literal: I composed the Biography of Bai Qi and Wang Jian as the thirteenth biography.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Biography of Bai Qi and Wang Jian as the thirteenth biography.

sentences.zh: 獵儒墨之遺文，明禮義之統紀，絕惠王利端，列往世興衰。

translations.literal: He gathered the surviving writings of the Confucians and Mohists, illuminated the guiding principles of rites and righteousness, cut off King Hui's pursuit of profit, and set forth the rise and fall of past ages.
translations.idiomatic: He gathered the surviving writings of the Confucians and Mohists, illuminated the guiding principles of ritual and righteousness, rejected King Hui's fixation on profit, and laid out the rise and fall of past generations.

sentences.zh: 作孟子荀卿列傳第十四。

translations.literal: I composed the Biography of Mencius and Xunzi as the fourteenth biography.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Biography of Mencius and Xunzi as the fourteenth biography.

sentences.zh: 好客喜士，士歸于薛，為齊捍楚魏。

translations.literal: He loved to host guests and delight in gentlemen; men of talent flocked to Xue, and on behalf of Qi he defended against Chu and Wei.
translations.idiomatic: A generous host who delighted in men of talent, he attracted scholars to Xue, and on Qi's behalf he held off Chu and Wei.

sentences.zh: 作孟嘗君列傳第十五。

translations.literal: I composed the Biography of Lord Mengchang as the fifteenth biography.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Biography of Lord Mengchang as the fifteenth biography.

sentences.zh: 爭馮亭以權，如楚以救邯鄲之圍，使其君復稱於諸侯。

translations.literal: He contended for Feng Ting through strategic calculation, went to Chu to lift the siege of Handan, and enabled his lord to be honored once more among the feudal lords.
translations.idiomatic: He contended for Feng Ting through shrewd calculation, traveled to Chu to lift the siege of Handan, and restored his lord's standing among the feudal lords.

sentences.zh: 作平原君虞卿列傳第十六。

translations.literal: I composed the Biography of Lord Pingyuan and Yu Qing as the sixteenth biography.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Biography of Lord Pingyuan and Yu Qing as the sixteenth biography.

sentences.zh: 能以富貴下貧賤，賢能詘於不肖，唯信陵君為能行之。

translations.literal: To humble oneself in wealth and honor before the poor and lowly, for a worthy man to defer to the unworthy — only Lord Xinling was able to practice this.
translations.idiomatic: To humble oneself, despite wealth and rank, before the poor and lowly; for a man of worth to defer to the unworthy — only Lord Xinling truly practiced this.

sentences.zh: 作魏公子列傳第十七。

translations.literal: I composed the Biography of the Prince of Wei as the seventeenth biography.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Biography of the Prince of Wei as the seventeenth biography.

sentences.zh: 以身徇君，遂脫彊秦，使馳說之士南鄉走楚者，黃歇之義。

translations.literal: To risk one's life for one's lord, to extricate him from mighty Qin, and to cause the itinerant persuaders to turn south and hasten to Chu — this was the righteousness of Huang Xie.
translations.idiomatic: Risking his own life for his lord, rescuing him from the clutches of mighty Qin, and causing the traveling persuaders to turn south and hasten to Chu — such was the righteousness of Huang Xie.

sentences.zh: 作春申君列傳第十八

translations.literal: I composed the Biography of Lord Chunshen as the eighteenth biography.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Biography of Lord Chunshen as the eighteenth biography.

sentences.zh: 能忍於魏齊，而信威於彊秦，推賢讓位，二子有之。

translations.literal: To endure humiliation under Wei Qi, yet to establish credible authority in mighty Qin; to recommend the worthy and yield one's position — these two men achieved it.
translations.idiomatic: Enduring humiliation under Wei Qi, yet establishing authority in mighty Qin; recommending the worthy and yielding one's position — these two men embodied both qualities.

sentences.zh: 作范睢蔡澤列傳第十九。

translations.literal: I composed the Biography of Fan Sui and Cai Ze as the nineteenth biography.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Biography of Fan Sui and Cai Ze as the nineteenth biography.

sentences.zh: 率行其謀，連五國兵，為弱燕報彊齊之讎，雪其先君之恥。

translations.literal: He carried out his plans decisively, united the armies of five states, avenged weak Yan's grudge against mighty Qi, and washed away his former lord's shame.
translations.idiomatic: He carried out his plans decisively, uniting the armies of five states to avenge weak Yan against mighty Qi and wash away his former lord's shame.

sentences.zh: 作樂毅列傳第二十。

translations.literal: I composed the Biography of Yue Yi as the twentieth biography.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Biography of Yue Yi as the twentieth biography.

sentences.zh: 能信意彊秦，而屈體廉子，用徇其君，俱重於諸侯。

translations.literal: Able to face mighty Qin with confidence, yet humble himself before Lian Po — both devoted themselves to their lord and were honored among the feudal lords.
translations.idiomatic: He faced mighty Qin with unflinching resolve, yet humbled himself before Lian Po. Both devoted themselves utterly to their lord and earned the respect of all the feudal lords.

sentences.zh: 作廉頗藺相如列傳第二十一。

translations.literal: I composed the Biography of Lian Po and Lin Xiangru as the twenty-first biography.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Biography of Lian Po and Lin Xiangru as the twenty-first biography.

sentences.zh: 湣王既失臨淄而奔莒，唯田單用即墨破走騎劫，遂存齊社稷。

translations.literal: After King Min lost Linzi and fled to Ju, only Tian Dan, using Jimo as his base, routed and drove away Qi Jie, thereby preserving the altars of state of Qi.
translations.idiomatic: After King Min lost Linzi and fled to Ju, Tian Dan alone used the fortress of Jimo to rout Qi Jie and drive him away, thereby preserving the altars of state of Qi.

sentences.zh: 作田單列傳第二十二。

translations.literal: I composed the Biography of Tian Dan as the twenty-second biography.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Biography of Tian Dan as the twenty-second biography.

sentences.zh: 能設詭說解患於圍城，輕爵祿，樂肆志。

translations.literal: He was able to devise ingenious arguments to relieve the distress of a besieged city, took rank and salary lightly, and delighted in giving free rein to his aspirations.
translations.idiomatic: He devised ingenious arguments to relieve a besieged city, treated rank and salary lightly, and delighted in giving free rein to his ambitions.

sentences.zh: 作魯仲連鄒陽列傳第二十三。

translations.literal: I composed the Biography of Lu Zhonglian and Zou Yang as the twenty-third biography.
translations.idiomatic: And so I composed the Biography of Lu Zhonglian and Zou Yang as the twenty-third biography.

sentences.zh: 作辭以諷諫，連類以爭義，離騷有之。

translations.literal: Composing verse to remonstrate through allegory, drawing analogies to argue for righteousness — the Li Sao embodies this.
translations.idiomatic: Composing verse to admonish through allegory, weaving analogies to argue for righteousness — this is the spirit of the Li Sao.

sentences.zh: 作屈原賈生列傳第二十四。
結子楚親，使諸侯之士斐然爭入事秦。
作呂不韋列傳第二十五。
曹子匕首，魯獲其田，齊明其信；
豫讓義不為二心。
作刺客列傳第二十六。
能明其畫，因時推秦，遂得意於海內，斯為謀首。
作李斯列傳第二十七。
為秦開地益眾，北靡匈奴，據河為塞，因山為固，建榆中。
作蒙恬列傳第二十八。
填趙塞常山以廣河內，弱楚權，明漢王之信於天下。
作張耳陳餘列傳第二十九。
收西河、上黨之兵，從至彭城；
越之侵掠梁地以苦項羽。
作魏豹彭越列傳第三十。
以淮南叛楚歸漢，漢用得大司馬殷，卒破子羽于垓下。
作黥布列傳第三十一。
楚人迫我京索，而信拔魏趙，定燕齊，使漢三分天下有其二，以滅項籍。
作淮陰侯列傳第三十二。
楚漢相距鞏洛，而韓信為填潁川，盧綰絕籍糧餉。
作韓信盧綰列傳第三十三。
諸侯畔項王，唯齊連子羽城陽，漢得以閒遂入彭城。
作田儋列傳第三十四。
攻城野戰，獲功歸報，噲、商有力焉，非獨鞭策，又與之脫難。
作樊酈列傳第三十五。
漢既初定，文理未明，蒼為主計，整齊度量，序律歷。
作張丞相列傳第三十六。
結言通使，約懷諸侯；
諸侯咸親，歸漢為藩輔。
作酈生陸賈列傳第三十七。
欲詳知秦楚之事，維周∴從高祖，平定諸侯。
作傅靳蒯成列傳第三十八。
徙彊族，都關中，和約匈奴；
明朝廷禮，次宗廟儀法。
作劉敬叔孫通列傳第三十九。
能摧剛作柔，卒為列臣；
欒公不劫於埶而倍死。
作季布欒布列傳第四十。
敢犯顏色以達主義，不顧其身，為國家樹長畫。
作袁盎晁錯列傳第四十一。
守法不失大理，言古賢人，增主之明。
作張釋之馮唐列傳第四十二。
敦厚慈孝，訥於言，敏於行，務在鞠躬，君子長者。
作萬石張叔列傳第四十三。
守節切直，義足以言廉，行足以厲賢，任重權不可以非理撓。
作田叔列傳第四十四。
扁鵲言醫，為方者宗，守數精明；
後世（修）［循］序，弗能易也，而倉公可謂近之矣。
作扁鵲倉公列傳第四十五。
維仲之省，厥濞王吳，遭漢初定，以填撫江淮之閒。
作吳王濞列傳第四十六。
吳楚為亂，宗屬唯嬰賢而喜士，士鄉之，率師抗山東滎陽。
作魏其武安列傳第四十七。
智足以應近世之變，寬足用得人。
作韓長孺列傳第四十八。
勇於當敵，仁愛士卒，號令不煩，師徒鄉之。
作李將軍列傳第四十九。
自三代以來，匈奴常為中國患害；
欲知彊弱之時，設備征討，作匈奴列傳第五十。
直曲塞，廣河南，破祁連，通西國，靡北胡。
作衛將軍驃騎列傳第五十一。
大臣宗室以侈靡相高，唯弘用節衣食為百吏先。
作平津侯列傳第五十二。
漢既平中國，而佗能集楊越以保南藩，納貢職。
作南越列傳第五十三。
吳之叛逆，甌人斬濞，葆守封禺為臣。
作東越列傳第五十四。
燕丹散亂遼閒，滿收其亡民，厥聚海東，以集真藩，葆塞為外臣。
作朝鮮列傳第五十五。
唐蒙使略通夜郎，而邛笮之君請為內臣受吏。
作西南夷列傳第五十六。
子虛之事，大人賦說，靡麗多誇，然其指風諫，歸於無為。
作司馬相如列傳第五十七。
黥布叛逆，子長國之，以填江淮之南，安剽楚庶民。
作淮南衡山列傳第五十八。
奉法循理之吏，不伐功矜能，百姓無稱，亦無過行。
作循吏列傳第五十九。
正衣冠立於朝廷，而群臣莫敢言浮說，長孺矜焉；
好薦人，稱長者，壯有溉。
作汲鄭列傳第六十。
自孔子卒，京師莫崇庠序，唯建元元狩之閒，文辭粲如也。
作儒林列傳第六十一。
民倍本多巧，姦軌弄法，善人不能化，唯一切嚴削為能齊之。
作酷吏列傳第六十二。
漢既通使大夏，而西極遠蠻，引領內鄉，欲觀中國。
作大宛列傳第六十三。
救人於緦振人不贍，仁者有乎；
不既信，不倍言，義者有取焉。
作游俠列傳第六十四。
夫事人君能說主耳目，和主顏色，而獲親近，非獨色愛，能亦各有所長。
作佞幸列傳第六十五。
不流世俗，不爭埶利，上下無所凝滯，人莫之害，以道之用。
作滑稽列傳第六十六。
齊、楚、秦、趙為日者，各有俗所用。
欲循觀其大旨，作日者列傳第六十七。
三王不同龜，四夷各異卜，然各以決吉凶。
略闚其要，作龜策列傳第六十八。
布衣匹夫之人，不害於政，不妨百姓，取與以時而息財富，智者有采焉。
作貨殖列傳第六十九。
維我漢繼五帝末流，接三代（統）［絕］業。
周道廢，秦撥去古文，焚滅詩書，故明堂石室金匱玉版圖籍散亂。
於是漢興，蕭何次律令，韓信申軍法，張蒼為章程，叔孫通定禮儀，則文學彬彬稍進，詩書往往閒出矣。
自曹參薦蓋公言黃老，而賈生、晁錯明申、商，公孫弘以儒顯，百年之閒，天下遺文古事靡不畢集太史公。
太史公仍父子相續纂其職。
曰：「於戲！
余維先人嘗掌斯事，顯於唐虞，至于周，復典之，故司馬氏世主天官。
至於余乎，欽念哉！
欽念哉！」
罔羅天下放失舊聞，王跡所興，原始察終，見盛觀衰，論考之行事，略推三代，錄秦漢，上記軒轅，下至于茲，著十二本紀，既科條之矣。
并時異世，年差不明，作十表。
禮樂損益，律歷改易，兵權山川鬼神，天人之際，承敝通變，作八書。
二十八宿環北辰，三十輻共一轂，運行無窮，輔拂股肱之臣配焉，忠信行道，以奉主上，作三十世家。
扶義俶儻，不令己失時，立功名於天下，作七十列傳。
凡百三十篇，五十二萬六千五百字，為太史公書。
序略，以拾遺補闕，成一家之言，厥協六經異傳，整齊百家雜語，藏之名山，副在京師，俟後世聖人君子。
第七十。
太史公曰：余述歷黃帝以來至太初而訖，百三十篇。
